content.text:
let's build a plan to fix each one of these chunk by chunk Verdict
Partial Pass

Scope and Verification Boundary

Reviewed backend/frontend source, APIs, models, services, and tests under the repo root.
Excluded input sources under ./.tmp/ entirely (not used as evidence).
Did not run any Docker/container command.
Attempted documented non-Docker verification only:
Backend tests: failed to start in this environment (No module named pytest).
Frontend npm run test / npm run build: failed in this environment with spawn EPERM (Vite/esbuild startup).
Docker-based verification is documented but was not executed due instruction boundary.
Remains unconfirmed: full end-to-end runtime behavior in a provisioned environment.
Top Findings

Severity: High
Conclusion: Registration mutations are not restricted to student role.
Brief rationale: enroll/waitlist/drop use authenticated user but do not enforce STUDENT role.
Evidence: registration.py (line 81), registration.py (line 95), registration.py (line 101), registration_service.py (line 126), registration_service.py (line 178), registration_service.py (line 194), test_registration_api.py (line 243)
Impact: Unauthorized role can perform enrollment lifecycle writes.
Minimum actionable fix: Enforce role gate (STUDENT for self-service) on registration write routes and add explicit 403 tests for non-student roles.

Severity: High
Conclusion: Configurable notification trigger automation is missing (only manual dispatch exists).
Brief rationale: Prompt requires configurable reminder triggers (72/24/2h, assignment posted, grading complete); current implementation is dispatch/list/read only.
Evidence: messaging.py (line 18), messaging.py (line 44), messaging.py (line 69), messaging_service.py (line 22), models/messaging.py (line 10)
Impact: Core prompt-fit gap in notification workflow behavior.
Minimum actionable fix: Add trigger configuration + scheduler/executor for in-app reminders and tests for timing/dispatch logic.

Severity: Medium
Conclusion: Data-quality checks are not integrated into normal domain writes.
Brief rationale: Quality logic exists but is invoked via standalone /data-quality/validate-write, not enforced in course/review/finance/admin write flows.
Evidence: data_quality.py (line 19), data_quality.py (line 24), data_quality.py (line 36), data_quality_service.py (line 27), data_quality_service.py (line 100)
Impact: Problematic writes can bypass quarantine/quality controls.
Minimum actionable fix: Add centralized pre-write quality enforcement hooks to relevant business write paths.

Severity: Medium
Conclusion: Local non-Docker verification is currently unconfirmed in this environment.
Brief rationale: Docs are present, but test/build execution failed due environment constraints (pytest unavailable; Node EPERM spawn).
Evidence: README.md (line 33), README.md (line 38), README.md (line 39), README.md (line 42), README.md (line 43)
Impact: Runtime confidence boundary.
Minimum actionable fix: Add deterministic prerequisite checks and validated fallback path for Windows EPERM/local env setup.

Severity: Medium
Conclusion: Frontend test coverage is minimal for business workflows.
Brief rationale: Only one frontend test file exists and it only checks route redirects.
Evidence: App.test.tsx (line 34), App.test.tsx (line 35), App.test.tsx (line 50), package.json (line 10)
Impact: Weak regression protection for role workflows and failure handling.
Minimum actionable fix: Add integration tests per role dashboard and at least one E2E path.

Security Summary

authentication / login-state handling: Pass
Evidence: lockout and password policy in auth_service.py (line 11), security.py (line 36); session expiry in config.py (line 15), auth.py (line 43).
frontend route protection / route guards: Partial Pass
Evidence: protected routing exists in App.tsx (line 19), App.tsx (line 22), App.tsx (line 53).
page-level / feature-level access control: Partial Pass
Evidence: many domain role checks exist, but registration writes have no student-role gate (Finding #1).
sensitive information exposure: Partial Pass
Evidence: integration secrets are protected in integration_service.py (line 57); frontend stores bearer token in localStorage at AuthContext.tsx (line 35), AuthContext.tsx (line 69).
cache / state isolation after switching users: Partial Pass
Evidence: logout clears token/user state in AuthContext.tsx (line 74), AuthContext.tsx (line 83); full runtime confirmation not possible here.
Test Sufficiency Summary

Test Overview
Unit tests: present (backend unit_tests).
Component tests: minimal (single frontend file).
Page/route integration tests: backend API integration is broad; frontend page-flow integration is limited.
E2E tests: cannot confirm evidence of any.
Entry points: README.md (line 38), README.md (line 39), package.json (line 10).
Core Coverage
happy path: covered (backend domain API tests are extensive).
key failure paths: partial (many 401/403/409/422 checks, but gaps remain for registration role abuse and trigger scheduling).
security-critical coverage: partial (good auth/session/integration tests, missing explicit registration-role denial coverage).
Major Gaps
No test asserting non-student users get 403 on registration write endpoints.
No tests for configurable scheduled notification triggers (72/24/2h).
No frontend integration/E2E tests for end-to-end role workflows.
Final Test Verdict: Partial Pass
Engineering Quality Summary
Major architecture is modular and reasonably maintainable (routers/services/models/schemas split by domain). Logging/audit patterns are structured. Main delivery-confidence issues are requirement-integration gaps and authorization consistency, not structural code collapse.

Visual and Interaction Summary
Clearly applicable (frontend exists). UI structure is coherent with role-specific dashboards and baseline loading/error/success feedback. Full visual/interaction acceptance cannot be fully confirmed due frontend runtime failure in this environment (spawn EPERM).

Next Actions

Add strict role authorization on registration writes and corresponding negative tests.

Implement configurable notification trigger scheduling and coverage tests.

Integrate data-quality enforcement into real write paths, not only standalone validation endpoint.

Harden non-Docker verification path with prerequisite checks and proven Windows fallback.

Expand frontend tests to role workflow integration + one E2E journey.

Saved report: DELIVERY_ACCEPTANCE_PROJECT_ARCH_AUDIT.md

<content>1: from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
2: import hashlib
3: import json
4: 
5: from fastapi import HTTPException, status

7: from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

9: from app.core.authz import require_section_access
10: from app.models.admin import Course, RegistrationRound, Section, Term
11: from app.models.registration import AddDropRequest, Enrollment, EnrollmentStatus, RegistrationHistory, WaitlistEntry
12: from app.models.user import User
13: 
14: IDEMPOTENCY_WINDOW_HOURS = 24
15: 
16: 
17: def _utcnow() -> datetime:
18:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
19: 
20: 
21: def _request_hash(payload: dict) -> str:
22:     canonical = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"))
23:     return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
24: 
25: 
26: def _record_history(db: Session, student_id: int, section_id: int, event_type: str, details: str | None = None) -> None:
27:     db.add(RegistrationHistory(student_id=student_id, section_id=section_id, event_type=event_type, details=details))
28: 
29: 
30: def _to_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
31:     if value.tzinfo is None:
32:         return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
33:     return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
34: 
35: 
36: def _purge_expired_idempotency_key(db: Session, actor_id: int, operation: str, idempotency_key: str) -> None:
37:     cutoff = _utcnow() - timedelta(hours=IDEMPOTENCY_WINDOW_HOURS)
38:     rows = (
39:         db.query(AddDropRequest)
40:         .filter(
41:             AddDropRequest.actor_id == actor_id,
42:             AddDropRequest.operation == operation,
43:             AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == idempotency_key,
44:         )
45:         .all()
46:     )
47:     deleted_any = False
48:     for row in rows:
49:         if _to_utc(row.created_at) < cutoff:
50:             db.delete(row)
51:             deleted_any = True
52:     if deleted_any:
53:         db.flush()
54: 
55: 
56: def active_round_for_term(db: Session, term_id: int) -> RegistrationRound | None:
57:     now = _utcnow()
58:     rounds = db.query(RegistrationRound).filter(RegistrationRound.term_id == term_id, RegistrationRound.is_active.is_(True)).all()
59:     for round_item in rounds:
60:         starts = round_item.starts_at if round_item.starts_at.tzinfo else round_item.starts_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
61:         ends = round_item.ends_at if round_item.ends_at.tzinfo else round_item.ends_at.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
62:         if starts <= now <= ends:
63:             return round_item
64:     return None
65: 
66: 
67: def check_eligibility(db: Session, student: User, course_id: int, section_id: int) -> list[str]:
68:     require_section_access(db, student, section_id)
69:     reasons: list[str] = []
70:     course = db.query(Course).filter(Course.id == course_id).first()
71:     section = db.query(Section).filter(Section.id == section_id, Section.course_id == course_id).first()
72:     if course is None or section is None:
73:         return ["Course or section not found."]
74: 
75:     term = db.query(Term).filter(Term.id == section.term_id).first()
76:     if term is None:
77:         reasons.append("Section term is invalid.")
78:     else:
79:         if active_round_for_term(db, term.id) is None:
80:             reasons.append("No active registration round for this section.")
81: 
82:     for prereq_code in course.prerequisites or []:
83:         completed = (
84:             db.query(Enrollment)
85:             .join(Section, Enrollment.section_id == Section.id)
86:             .join(Course, Section.course_id == Course.id)
87:             .filter(Enrollment.student_id == student.id, Enrollment.status == EnrollmentStatus.completed, Course.code == prereq_code)
88:             .first()
89:         )
90:         if completed is None:
91:             reasons.append(f"Missing prerequisite: {prereq_code}.")
92: 
93:     return reasons
94: 
95: 
96: def _current_enrolled_count(db: Session, section_id: int) -> int:
97:     return (
98:         db.query(func.count(Enrollment.id))
99:         .filter(Enrollment.section_id == section_id, Enrollment.status == EnrollmentStatus.enrolled)
100:         .scalar()
101:     )
102: 
103: 
104: def _consume_waitlist_if_seat_available(db: Session, section_id: int) -> None:
105:     section = db.query(Section).filter(Section.id == section_id).first()
106:     if section is None:
107:         return
108:     while _current_enrolled_count(db, section_id) < section.capacity:
109:         next_wait = db.query(WaitlistEntry).filter(WaitlistEntry.section_id == section_id).order_by(WaitlistEntry.priority.asc()).first()
110:         if next_wait is None:
111:             break
112:         enrollment = (
113:             db.query(Enrollment)
114:             .filter(Enrollment.student_id == next_wait.student_id, Enrollment.section_id == section_id)
115:             .first()
116:         )
117:         if enrollment is None:
118:             enrollment = Enrollment(student_id=next_wait.student_id, section_id=section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.enrolled)
119:             db.add(enrollment)
120:         else:
121:             enrollment.status = EnrollmentStatus.enrolled
122:         _record_history(db, next_wait.student_id, section_id, "WAITLIST_BACKFILLED", "Auto promoted from waitlist")
123:         db.delete(next_wait)
124:

126: def enroll(db: Session, student: User, section_id: int, idempotency_key: str) -> tuple[int, dict]:
127:     require_section_access(db, student, section_id)
128:     payload = {"section_id": section_id}
129:     hash_value = _request_hash(payload)
130:     _purge_expired_idempotency_key(db, student.id, "ENROLL", idempotency_key)
131:     existing_request = (
132:         db.query(AddDropRequest)
133:         .filter(AddDropRequest.actor_id == student.id, AddDropRequest.operation == "ENROLL", AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == idempotency_key)
134:         .first()
135:     )
136:     if existing_request is not None:
137:         if existing_request.request_hash != hash_value:
138:             raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Idempotency key conflict for different request payload.")
139:         return existing_request.response_code, json.loads(existing_request.response_body)
140: 
141:     section = db.query(Section).filter(Section.id == section_id).with_for_update().first()
142:     if section is None:
143:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Section not found.")
144:     reasons = check_eligibility(db, student, section.course_id, section_id)
145:     if reasons:
146:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail={"eligible": False, "reasons": reasons})
147: 
148:     existing = db.query(Enrollment).filter(Enrollment.student_id == student.id, Enrollment.section_id == section_id).first()
149:     if existing and existing.status == EnrollmentStatus.enrolled:
150:         response = {"status": "already_enrolled", "section_id": section_id}
151:         code = 200
152:     elif _current_enrolled_count(db, section_id) >= section.capacity:
153:         code = 409
154:         response = {"status": "full", "section_id": section_id}
155:     else:
156:         if existing is None:
157:             db.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.enrolled))
158:         else:
159:             existing.status = EnrollmentStatus.enrolled
160:         _record_history(db, student.id, section_id, "ENROLLED")
161:         code = 200
162:         response = {"status": "enrolled", "section_id": section_id}
163: 
164:     db.add(
165:         AddDropRequest(
166:             actor_id=student.id,
167:             operation="ENROLL",
168:             idempotency_key=idempotency_key,
169:             request_hash=hash_value,
170:             response_code=code,
171:             response_body=json.dumps(response),
172:         )
173:     )
174:     db.commit()
175:     return code, response
176:

178: def join_waitlist(db: Session, student: User, section_id: int) -> dict:
179:     require_section_access(db, student, section_id)
180:     section = db.query(Section).filter(Section.id == section_id).first()
181:     if section is None:
182:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Section not found.")
183:     existing = db.query(WaitlistEntry).filter(WaitlistEntry.student_id == student.id, WaitlistEntry.section_id == section_id).first()
184:     if existing is not None:
185:         return {"status": "already_waitlisted", "section_id": section_id, "priority": existing.priority}
186:     max_priority = db.query(func.max(WaitlistEntry.priority)).filter(WaitlistEntry.section_id == section_id).scalar()
187:     priority = int(max_priority or 0) + 1
188:     db.add(WaitlistEntry(student_id=student.id, section_id=section_id, priority=priority))
189:     _record_history(db, student.id, section_id, "WAITLIST_JOINED", f"priority={priority}")
190:     db.commit()
191:     return {"status": "waitlisted", "section_id": section_id, "priority": priority}
192: 
193: 
194: def drop(db: Session, student: User, section_id: int, idempotency_key: str) -> tuple[int, dict]:
195:     require_section_access(db, student, section_id)
196:     payload = {"section_id": section_id}
197:     hash_value = _request_hash(payload)
198:     _purge_expired_idempotency_key(db, student.id, "DROP", idempotency_key)
199:     existing_request = (
200:         db.query(AddDropRequest)
201:         .filter(AddDropRequest.actor_id == student.id, AddDropRequest.operation == "DROP", AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == idempotency_key)
202:         .first()
203:     )
204:     if existing_request is not None:
205:         if existing_request.request_hash != hash_value:
206:             raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Idempotency key conflict for different request payload.")
207:         return existing_request.response_code, json.loads(existing_request.response_body)
208: 
209:     enrollment = (
210:         db.query(Enrollment)
211:         .filter(Enrollment.student_id == student.id, Enrollment.section_id == section_id, Enrollment.status == EnrollmentStatus.enrolled)
212:         .first()
213:     )
214:     if enrollment is None:
215:         code = 404
216:         response = {"status": "not_enrolled", "section_id": section_id}
217:     else:
218:         enrollment.status = EnrollmentStatus.dropped
219:         _record_history(db, student.id, section_id, "DROPPED")
220:         db.flush()
221:         _consume_waitlist_if_seat_available(db, section_id)
222:         code = 200
223:         response = {"status": "dropped", "section_id": section_id}
224: 
225:     db.add(

<content>1: from datetime import datetime, timezone
2: import json
3: 
4: from fastapi import HTTPException
5: from sqlalchemy import desc, func
6: from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
7: 
8: from app.models.messaging import Notification, NotificationLog, NotificationTrigger
9: 
10: 
11: def _utcnow() -> datetime:
12:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
13: 
14: 
15: def _parse_trigger(trigger_type: str) -> NotificationTrigger:
16:     try:
17:         return NotificationTrigger(trigger_type)
18:     except ValueError as exc:
19:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="Unsupported trigger type.") from exc
20: 
21: 
22: def dispatch_notifications(
23:     db: Session,
24:     *,
25:     trigger_type: str,
26:     title: str,
27:     message: str,
28:     recipient_ids: list[int],
29:     metadata: dict | None = None,
30: ) -> dict:
31:     trigger = _parse_trigger(trigger_type)
32:     created_ids: list[int] = []
33: 
34:     for recipient_id in recipient_ids:
35:         notification = Notification(
36:             recipient_id=recipient_id,
37:             trigger_type=trigger,
38:             title=title,
39:             message=message,
40:             metadata_json=json.dumps(metadata, sort_keys=True, default=str) if metadata else None,
41:         )
42:         db.add(notification)
43:         db.flush()
44:         db.add(
45:             NotificationLog(
46:                 notification_id=notification.id,
47:                 recipient_id=recipient_id,
48:                 event_type="DELIVERED",
49:                 details=f"trigger={trigger.value}",
50:             )
51:         )
52:         created_ids.append(notification.id)
53: 
54:     db.commit()
55:     return {"created": len(created_ids), "notification_ids": created_ids}
56: 
57: 
58: def list_notifications(db: Session, recipient_id: int, limit: int = 50, offset: int = 0) -> tuple[int, list[Notification]]:
59:     unread_count = (
60:         db.query(func.count(Notification.id))
61:         .filter(Notification.recipient_id == recipient_id, Notification.read.is_(False))
62:         .scalar()
63:     )
64:     rows = (
65:         db.query(Notification)
66:         .filter(Notification.recipient_id == recipient_id)
67:         .order_by(desc(Notification.id))
68:         .offset(offset)
69:         .limit(limit)
70:         .all()
71:     )
72:     return int(unread_count or 0), rows
73: 
74: 
75: def mark_read(db: Session, notification_id: int, recipient_id: int) -> Notification:
76:     notification = (
77:         db.query(Notification)
78:         .filter(Notification.id == notification_id, Notification.recipient_id == recipient_id)
79:         .first()
80:     )
81:     if notification is None:
82:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Notification not found.")
83:     if not notification.read:
84:         notification.read = True
85:         notification.read_at = _utcnow()
86:         db.add(
87:             NotificationLog(
88:                 notification_id=notification.id,
89:                 recipient_id=recipient_id,
90:                 event_type="READ",
91:                 details=None,
92:             )
93:         )
94:         db.commit()
95:         db.refresh(notification)
96:     return notification

from datetime import datetime
import enum

from sqlalchemy import Boolean, DateTime, Enum, ForeignKey, Integer, String, Text, func
from sqlalchemy.orm import Mapped, mapped_column

from app.core.database import Base


class NotificationTrigger(str, enum.Enum):
    assignment_posted = "ASSIGNMENT_POSTED"
    deadline_72h = "DEADLINE_72H"
    deadline_24h = "DEADLINE_24H"
    deadline_2h = "DEADLINE_2H"
    grading_completed = "GRADING_COMPLETED"


class Notification(Base):
    __tablename__ = "notifications"

<content>1: from datetime import datetime, timezone
2: import hashlib
3: import json
4: from difflib import SequenceMatcher
5: 
6: from fastapi import HTTPException
7: from sqlalchemy import func
8: from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
9: 
10: from app.core.config import settings
11: from app.models.data_quality import QuarantineEntry, QuarantineStatus
12: 
13: 
14: def _utcnow() -> datetime:
15:     return datetime.now(timezone.utc)
16: 
17: 
18: def _fingerprint(payload: dict) -> str:
19:     canonical = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
20:     return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
21: 
22: 
23: def _similarity(a: str, b: str) -> float:
24:     return SequenceMatcher(None, a, b).ratio()
25: 
26: 
27: def evaluate_payload(
28:     db: Session,
29:     *,
30:     entity_type: str,
31:     payload: dict,
32:     required_fields: list[str],
33:     ranges: dict[str, dict[str, float]],
34:     unique_keys: list[str],
35: ) -> tuple[bool, int, list[str], str]:
36:     reasons: list[str] = []
37:     score = 100
38: 
39:     for field in required_fields:
40:         value = payload.get(field)
41:         if value is None or (isinstance(value, str) and not value.strip()):
42:             reasons.append(f"Missing required field: {field}")
43:             score -= 20
44: 
45:     for field, bounds in ranges.items():
46:         if field not in payload:
47:             continue
48:         try:
49:             value = float(payload[field])
50:         except (TypeError, ValueError):
51:             reasons.append(f"Range validation failed for {field}: non-numeric")
52:             score -= 15
53:             continue
54:         min_value = bounds.get("min")
55:         max_value = bounds.get("max")
56:         if min_value is not None and value < min_value:
57:             reasons.append(f"Range validation failed for {field}: below minimum")
58:             score -= 15
59:         if max_value is not None and value > max_value:
60:             reasons.append(f"Range validation failed for {field}: above maximum")
61:             score -= 15
62: 
63:     fingerprint = _fingerprint(payload)
64:     existing_fingerprint = (
65:         db.query(QuarantineEntry)
66:         .filter(QuarantineEntry.entity_type == entity_type, QuarantineEntry.fingerprint == fingerprint)
67:         .first()
68:     )
69:     if existing_fingerprint is not None:
70:         reasons.append("Duplicate fingerprint detected")
71:         score -= 20
72: 
73:     for key in unique_keys:
74:         if key not in payload:
75:             continue
76:         value = str(payload[key])
77:         similar_rows = (
78:             db.query(QuarantineEntry)
79:             .filter(QuarantineEntry.entity_type == entity_type)
80:             .order_by(QuarantineEntry.id.desc())
81:             .limit(50)
82:             .all()
83:         )
84:         for row in similar_rows:
85:             try:
86:                 row_payload = json.loads(row.payload_json)
87:             except json.JSONDecodeError:
88:                 continue
89:             candidate = str(row_payload.get(key, ""))
90:             if candidate and _similarity(value.lower(), candidate.lower()) >= settings.dedup_threshold:
91:                 reasons.append(f"Potential duplicate by similarity on '{key}'")
92:                 score -= 15
93:                 break
94: 
95:     score = max(0, min(100, score))
96:     accepted = len(reasons) == 0
97:     return accepted, score, reasons, fingerprint
98: 
99: 
100: def quarantine_write(
101:     db: Session,
102:     *,
103:     entity_type: str,
104:     payload: dict,
105:     reasons: list[str],
106:     quality_score: int,
107:     fingerprint: str,
108: ) -> QuarantineEntry:
109:     entry = QuarantineEntry(
110:         entity_type=entity_type,
111:         payload_json=json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, default=str),
112:         rejection_reason="; ".join(reasons),
113:         quality_score=quality_score,
114:         fingerprint=fingerprint,
115:         status=QuarantineStatus.open,
116:     )
117:     db.add(entry)
118:     db.commit()
119:     db.refresh(entry)
120:     return entry
121: 
122: 
123: def list_quarantine(db: Session, status: str | None, limit: int, offset: int) -> list[QuarantineEntry]:
124:     query = db.query(QuarantineEntry)
125:     if status:
126:         try:
127:             parsed = QuarantineStatus(status)
128:         except ValueError as exc:
129:             raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="Invalid status filter") from exc
130:         query = query.filter(QuarantineEntry.status == parsed)
131:     return query.order_by(QuarantineEntry.id.desc()).offset(offset).limit(limit).all()
132: 
133: 
134: def resolve_quarantine(db: Session, entry_id: int, action: str, resolver_id: int) -> QuarantineEntry:
135:     entry = db.query(QuarantineEntry).filter(QuarantineEntry.id == entry_id).first()
136:     if entry is None:
137:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Quarantine entry not found")
138:     action_upper = action.upper()
139:     if action_upper not in {"ACCEPT", "DISCARD"}:
140:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="Action must be ACCEPT or DISCARD")
141:     entry.status = QuarantineStatus.accepted if action_upper == "ACCEPT" else QuarantineStatus.discarded
142:     entry.resolved_at = _utcnow()
143:     entry.resolved_by = resolver_id
144:     db.commit()
145:     db.refresh(entry)
146:     return entry
147: 
148: 
149: def quality_report(db: Session) -> list[dict]:
150:     entity_types = [row[0] for row in db.query(QuarantineEntry.entity_type).distinct().all()]
151:     result: list[dict] = []
152:     for entity_type in entity_types:
153:         total = db.query(func.count(QuarantineEntry.id)).filter(QuarantineEntry.entity_type == entity_type).scalar() or 0
154:         open_items = (
155:             db.query(func.count(QuarantineEntry.id))
156:             .filter(QuarantineEntry.entity_type == entity_type, QuarantineEntry.status == QuarantineStatus.open)
157:             .scalar()
158:             or 0
159:         )
160:         avg_score = (
161:             db.query(func.avg(QuarantineEntry.quality_score))
162:             .filter(QuarantineEntry.entity_type == entity_type)
163:             .scalar()
164:         )
165:         result.append(
166:             {
167:                 "entity_type": entity_type,
168:                 "total_records": int(total),
169:                 "quarantined": int(total),
170:                 "open_items": int(open_items),
171:                 "avg_quality_score": round(float(avg_score or 0.0), 2),
172:             }
173:         )
174:     return result

from datetime import datetime, timezone
import hashlib
import json
from difflib import SequenceMatcher

from fastapi import HTTPException
from sqlalchemy import func
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.config import settings
from app.models.data_quality import QuarantineEntry, QuarantineStatus


def _utcnow() -> datetime:
    return datetime.now(timezone.utc)


def _fingerprint(payload: dict) -> str:
    canonical = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), default=str)
    return hashlib.sha256(canonical.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()

155: @router.post("/rounds/{round_id}/assignments/auto")
156: def auto_assign(
157:     round_id: int,
158:     payload: AutoAssignmentIn,
159:     db: Session = Depends(get_db),
160:     user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
161: ):
162:     _ensure_instructor_or_admin(user)
163:     round_obj = review_service._get_round(db, round_id)
164:     _ensure_round_scope(db, user, round_obj)
165:     reviewers = db.query(User).filter(User.role == UserRole.reviewer, User.is_active.is_(True)).all()
166:     if not reviewers:
167:         raise HTTPException(status_code=422, detail="No active reviewers available.")
168: 
169:     created = 0
170:     created_ids: list[int] = []
171:     pointer = 0
172:     for student_id in payload.student_ids:
173:         assigned_for_student = 0
174:         tried = 0
175:         while assigned_for_student < payload.reviewers_per_student and tried < len(reviewers) * 3:
176:             reviewer = reviewers[pointer % len(reviewers)]
177:             pointer += 1
178:             tried += 1
179:             try:
180:                 review_service._check_coi(db, round_obj, reviewer.id, student_id)
181:             except HTTPException:
182:                 continue
183:             exists = (
184:                 db.query(ReviewerAssignment)
185:                 .filter(ReviewerAssignment.round_id == round_id, ReviewerAssignment.reviewer_id == reviewer.id, ReviewerAssignment.student_id == student_id)
186:                 .first()
187:             )
188:             if exists:
189:                 continue
190:             assignment = ReviewerAssignment(
191:                 round_id=round_id,
192:                 reviewer_id=reviewer.id,
193:                 student_id=student_id,
194:                 section_id=round_obj.section_id,
195:                 assigned_manually=False,
196:             )
197:             db.add(assignment)
198:             db.flush()
199:             created_ids.append(assignment.id)
200:             created += 1
201:             assigned_for_student += 1
202:         if assigned_for_student < payload.reviewers_per_student:
203:             raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail=f"Insufficient eligible reviewers for student {student_id} due to conflicts.")
204:     write_audit_log(
205:         db,
206:         actor_id=user.id,
207:         action="review.assignment.auto.create",
208:         entity_name="ReviewerAssignment",
209:         entity_id=None,
210:         before=None,
211:         after={"created_count": created, "assignment_ids": created_ids, "round_id": round_id},
212:     )
213:     db.commit()
214:     return {"created_assignments": created}

235: def submit_score(payload: ScoreSubmitIn, db: Session = Depends(get_db), user: User = Depends(get_current_user)):
236:     assignment = db.query(ReviewerAssignment).filter(ReviewerAssignment.id == payload.assignment_id).first()
237:     if assignment is None:
238:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Assignment not found.")
239:     if user.role != UserRole.reviewer or assignment.reviewer_id != user.id:
240:         raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="Cannot submit score for unassigned work.")
241: 
242:     round_obj = review_service._get_round(db, assignment.round_id)
243:     if round_obj.status == ReviewRoundStatus.closed:
244:         raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="Round is closed.")
245:     form = db.query(ScoringForm).filter(ScoringForm.id == round_obj.scoring_form_id).first()
246:     if form is None:
247:         raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Scoring form not found.")
248: 
249:     total = review_service._calculate_total_score(form, payload.criterion_scores)
250:     existing = db.query(Score).filter(Score.assignment_id == assignment.id).first()
251:     action = "review.score.update" if existing else "review.score.create"
252:     before_score = None
253:     if existing:
254:         before_score = {
255:             "id": existing.id,
256:             "assignment_id": existing.assignment_id,
257:             "total_score": existing.total_score,
258:             "comment": existing.comment,
259:         }
260:     if existing:
261:         db.query(Score).filter(Score.id == existing.id).update(
262:             {
263:                 "criterion_scores": payload.criterion_scores,
264:                 "total_score": total,
265:                 "comment": payload.comment if payload.comment is not None else "",
266:             }
267:         )
268:         db.flush()
269:         db.refresh(existing)
270:         score = existing
271:     else:
272:         score = Score(
273:             assignment_id=assignment.id,
274:             criterion_scores=payload.criterion_scores,
275:             total_score=total,
276:             comment=payload.comment if payload.comment is not None else "",
277:         )
278:         db.add(score)
279:         db.flush()
280: 
281:     review_service._evaluate_outliers(db, assignment.round_id, assignment.student_id)
282:     write_audit_log(
283:         db,
284:         actor_id=user.id,
285:         action=action,
286:         entity_name="Score",
287:         entity_id=score.id,
288:         before=before_score,
289:         after={
290:             "id": score.id,
291:             "assignment_id": score.assignment_id,
292:             "round_id": assignment.round_id,
293:             "student_id": assignment.student_id,
294:             "reviewer_id": assignment.reviewer_id,
295:             "total_score": score.total_score,
296:         },
297:     )
298:     logger.info(
299:         "review_score_written",
300:         extra={
301:             "event": "reviews.score.written",
302:             "fields": {
303:                 "action": action,
304:                 "score_id": score.id,
305:                 "assignment_id": assignment.id,
306:                 "round_id": assignment.round_id,
307:                 "reviewer_id": assignment.reviewer_id,
308:             },
309:         },
310:     )
311:     db.commit()
312:     db.refresh(score)
313:     return ScoreOut(id=score.id, assignment_id=score.assignment_id, total_score=score.total_score, submitted_at=score.submitted_at)

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.security import hash_password
from app.models.user import User, UserRole


def _create_user(db: Session, username: str, role: UserRole, password: str) -> User:
    user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password(password), role=role, is_active=True)
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(user)
    return user


def _login(client, username: str, password: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    response = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": username, "password": password})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {response.json()['token']}"}

<content>1: # Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System
2: 
3: ## Services
4: - `db`: PostgreSQL 15
5: - `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
6: - `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)
7: 
8: ## Quick Start (Docker)
9: 1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
10: 2. Ensure required env vars are set:
11:    - `DATABASE_URL`
12:    - `SECRET_KEY`
13:    - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
14: 3. Build and start services:
15:    - `docker compose up --build -d`
16: 4. Check API health:
17:    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
18:    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
19: 5. Open frontend:
20:    - `http://localhost:5173`
21: 
22: ## Local Setup (No Docker)
23: - Backend prerequisites:
24:   - Python 3.11+
25:   - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
26:     - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
27:     - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
28:   - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
29: - Frontend prerequisites:
30:   - Node.js 20+
31:   - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`
32: 
33: ## Run Tests
34: - Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
35: - Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
36: - Local backend (no Docker):
37:   - `cd backend`
38:   - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
39:   - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
40: - Local frontend checks (no Docker):
41:   - `cd frontend`
42:   - `npm run test`
43:   - `npm run build`
44: 
45: ## Troubleshooting
46: - `No module named pytest`:
47:   - Activate your backend virtual environment.
48:   - Reinstall backend deps with `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`.
49: - `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
50:   - Reinstall deps with `npm ci`.
51:   - Ensure no process is locking `node_modules/.bin`.
52:   - If needed, remove `frontend/node_modules` and reinstall.
53: 
54: ## Notes
55: - The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
56: - The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
57: - Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

(End of file - total 57 lines)
</content>

2: 
3: import { Box, CircularProgress } from "@mui/material";
4: import { Navigate, Route, Routes, useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
5: 
6: import { useAuth } from "./contexts/AuthContext";
7: 
8: const AppPortal = lazy(async () => import("./pages/AppPortal").then((module) => ({ default: module.AppPortal })));
9: const LoginPage = lazy(async () => import("./pages/LoginPage").then((module) => ({ default: module.LoginPage })));
10: 
11: function CenterLoader() {
12:   return (
13:     <Box sx={{ minHeight: "100vh", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center" }}>
14:       <CircularProgress />
15:     </Box>
16:   );
17: }
18: 
19: function ProtectedApp() {
20:   const { user, token, logout } = useAuth();
21:   if (!user || !token) {
22:     return <Navigate to="/login" replace />;
23:   }
24:   return (
25:     <Suspense fallback={<CenterLoader />}>
26:       <AppPortal token={token} role={user.role} username={user.username} onLogout={logout} />
27:     </Suspense>
28:   );
29: }
30: 
31: function LoginRoute() {
32:   const navigate = useNavigate();
33:   const { isAuthenticated } = useAuth();
34:   if (isAuthenticated) {
35:     return <Navigate to="/app" replace />;
36:   }
37:   return (
38:     <Suspense fallback={<CenterLoader />}>
39:       <LoginPage onSuccess={() => navigate("/app", { replace: true })} />
40:     </Suspense>
41:   );
42: }
43: 
44: export function App() {
45:   const { isBootstrapping } = useAuth();
46:   if (isBootstrapping) {
47:     return <CenterLoader />;
48:   }
49: 
50:   return (
51:     <Routes>
52:       <Route path="/login" element={<LoginRoute />} />
53:       <Route path="/app" element={<ProtectedApp />} />
54:       <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/app" replace />} />
55:     </Routes>
56:   );
57: }

<content>1: import { lazy, Suspense, useMemo, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import { getNotifications, markNotificationRead } from "../api/messaging";
4: import { AppShell } from "../components/AppShell";
5: import { NotificationsDrawer } from "../components/NotificationsDrawer";
6: import { useAsyncResource } from "../hooks/useAsyncResource";
7: import { Role } from "../types";
8: import { LoadingBlock } from "../components/StateBlock";
9: 
10: const AdminDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/AdminDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.AdminDashboard })));
11: const DataQualityDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/DataQualityDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.DataQualityDashboard })));
12: const FinanceDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/FinanceDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.FinanceDashboard })));
13: const ReviewerDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/ReviewerDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.ReviewerDashboard })));
14: const StudentDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/StudentDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.StudentDashboard })));
15: 
16: type AppPortalProps = {
17:   token: string;
18:   role: Role;
19:   username: string;
20:   onLogout: () => Promise<void>;
21: };
22: 
23: type ViewKey = "overview" | "data-quality";
24: 
25: export function AppPortal({ token, role, username, onLogout }: AppPortalProps) {
26:   const [view, setView] = useState<ViewKey>("overview");
27:   const [notifOpen, setNotifOpen] = useState(false);
28: 
29:   const notifications = useAsyncResource(() => getNotifications(token), [token]);
30: 
31:   const roleTitle = useMemo(() => {
32:     if (role === "ADMIN") {
33:       return "Administrator Dashboard";
34:     }
35:     if (role === "STUDENT") {
36:       return "Student Workspace";
37:     }
38:     if (role === "INSTRUCTOR") {
39:       return "Instructor Workbench";
40:     }
41:     if (role === "REVIEWER") {
42:       return "Reviewer Workbench";
43:     }
44:     return "Finance Desk";
45:   }, [role]);
46: 
47:   const navItems =
48:     role === "ADMIN"
49:       ? [
50:           { label: "Overview", active: view === "overview", onClick: () => setView("overview") },
51:           { label: "Data Quality", active: view === "data-quality", onClick: () => setView("data-quality") }
52:         ]
53:       : [{ label: "Overview", active: true, onClick: () => setView("overview") }];
54: 
55:   const handleMarkRead = async (id: number) => {
56:     await markNotificationRead(token, id);
57:     await notifications.reload();
58:   };
59: 
60:   const renderOverview = () => {
61:     if (role === "ADMIN") {
62:       if (view === "data-quality") {
63:         return <DataQualityDashboard token={token} />;
64:       }
65:       return <AdminDashboard token={token} />;
66:     }
67:     if (role === "STUDENT") {
68:       return <StudentDashboard token={token} />;
69:     }
70:     if (role === "REVIEWER") {
71:       return <ReviewerDashboard token={token} roleLabel="Reviewer" />;
72:     }
73:     if (role === "INSTRUCTOR") {
74:       return <ReviewerDashboard token={token} roleLabel="Instructor" />;
75:     }
76:     return <FinanceDashboard token={token} />;
77:   };
78: 
79:   return (
80:     <>
81:       <AppShell
82:         title={roleTitle}
83:         subtitle={`Signed in as ${username}`}
84:         navItems={navItems}
85:         unreadCount={notifications.data?.unread_count ?? 0}
86:         onNotificationsClick={() => setNotifOpen(true)}
87:         onLogout={() => {
88:           void onLogout();
89:         }}
90:       >
91:         <Suspense fallback={<LoadingBlock label="Loading workspace" />}>
92:           {renderOverview()}
93:         </Suspense>
94:       </AppShell>
95:       <NotificationsDrawer
96:         open={notifOpen}
97:         onClose={() => setNotifOpen(false)}
98:         items={notifications.data?.notifications ?? []}
99:         onMarkRead={(id) => {
100:           void handleMarkRead(id);
101:         }}
102:       />
103:     </>
104:   );
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>

19: def create_app() -> FastAPI:
20:     configure_logging()
21:     logger = get_logger("app.main")
22:     app = FastAPI(title="CEMS API", version="0.1.0")
23: 
24:     app.add_middleware(
25:         CORSMiddleware,
26:         allow_origins=["*"],
27:         allow_credentials=False,
28:         allow_methods=["*"],
29:         allow_headers=["*"],
30:     )
31: 
32:     @app.middleware("http")
33:     async def request_context_middleware(request: Request, call_next):
34:         request_id = ensure_request_id(request.headers.get("X-Request-ID"))
35:         try:
36:             response = await call_next(request)
37:             response.headers["X-Request-ID"] = request_id
38:             logger.info(
39:                 "request_completed",
40:                 extra={
41:                     "event": "http.request.completed",
42:                     "fields": {
43:                         "method": request.method,
44:                         "path": request.url.path,
45:                         "status_code": response.status_code,
46:                     },
47:                 },
48:             )
49:             return response
50:         except Exception:
51:             logger.exception(
52:                 "request_failed",
53:                 extra={
54:                     "event": "http.request.failed",
55:                     "fields": {
56:                         "method": request.method,
57:                         "path": request.url.path,
58:                     },
59:                 },
60:             )
61:             return JSONResponse(status_code=500, content={"detail": "Internal server error."}, headers={"X-Request-ID": request_id})
62:         finally:
63:             clear_request_id()
64: 
65:     router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v1")
66: 
67:     @router.get("/health/live", tags=["health"])
68:     def live() -> dict[str, str]:
69:         return {"status": "ok", "service": "api", "env": settings.environment}
70: 
71:     @router.get("/health/ready", tags=["health"])
72:     def ready() -> dict[str, str]:
73:         check_db_connection()
74:         return {"status": "ready"}
75: 
76:     app.include_router(router)
77:     app.include_router(auth_router, prefix="/api/v1")
78:     app.include_router(admin_router, prefix="/api/v1")
79:     app.include_router(registration_router, prefix="/api/v1")

84:     app.include_router(integrations_router, prefix="/api/v1")
85:     return app

from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class ValidateWriteIn(BaseModel):
    entity_type: str = Field(min_length=2, max_length=80)

    required_fields: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)
    ranges: dict[str, dict[str, float]] = Field(default_factory=dict)
    unique_keys: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list)


class ValidateWriteOut(BaseModel):
    accepted: bool
    quality_score: int
    reasons: list[str]
    quarantine_id: int | None = None

Great call. HereΓÇÖs a chunked execution plan that maps directly to the audit findings and keeps risk low.

**Chunk 1: Lock registration writes to students (High)**
- Scope: enforce STUDENT-only on self-service write endpoints in `backend/app/routers/registration.py` and add defense-in-depth checks in `backend/app/services/registration_service.py`.
- Changes:
  - Add a small role gate helper and apply it to `/registration/enroll`, `/registration/waitlist`, `/registration/drop`.
  - Keep section-scope checks as-is (role gate first, then scope).
- Tests:
  - Extend `backend/API_tests/test_registration_api.py` with explicit 403 for non-student roles (admin/instructor/finance_clerk) on all 3 write routes.
  - Keep existing cross-org scope tests intact.
- Done when:
  - Non-students get 403 on registration writes.
  - Students still pass enroll/waitlist/drop happy paths.

**Chunk 2: Add configurable trigger automation with in-app poller (High)**
- Scope: implement configurable notification triggers + scheduler for 72h/24h/2h, assignment posted, grading completed.
- Changes:
  - Add trigger config and delivery scheduling models (new migration under `backend/alembic/versions` + model updates in `backend/app/models/messaging.py`).
  - Add config/query APIs in `backend/app/routers/messaging.py` + schemas in `backend/app/schemas/messaging.py`.
  - Add scheduler/executor services in `backend/app/services/messaging_service.py` and startup poller wiring in `backend/app/main.py` (your selected approach).
  - Hook event producers (e.g. review round publish/close paths) in `backend/app/routers/reviews.py`.
- Tests:
  - Extend `backend/API_tests/test_messaging_api.py` for:
    - trigger config CRUD/rbac,
    - due-time dispatch correctness for 72/24/2h,
    - assignment/grading event-triggered notification creation.
- Done when:
  - Trigger configs can be enabled/disabled and are respected.
  - Due notifications are auto-dispatched by poller without manual API calls.

**Chunk 3: Integrate data-quality checks into real write paths (Medium)**
- Scope: move from standalone validate endpoint to pre-write enforcement in domain writes.
- Changes:
  - Add reusable guard/helper in `backend/app/services/data_quality_service.py` that evaluates payload and quarantines/rejects consistently.
  - Apply hooks to representative write routes first:
    - admin writes in `backend/app/routers/admin.py` (org/course/section/user create/update),
    - finance write routes in `backend/app/routers/finance.py`,
    - review write routes in `backend/app/routers/reviews.py`.
  - Keep `/data-quality/validate-write` for explicit checks, but route internals through shared helper to avoid drift.
- Tests:
  - Extend `backend/API_tests/test_data_quality_api.py` plus domain API tests (`test_admin_api.py`, `test_finance_api.py`, `test_reviews_api.py`) for reject/quarantine behavior.
- Done when:
  - Bad payloads canΓÇÖt be committed through normal business endpoints.
  - Quarantine entries + audit logs are generated consistently.

**Chunk 4: Harden non-Docker local verification path (Medium)**
- Scope: make local verification deterministic in Windows environments (pytest missing / EPERM issues).
- Changes:
  - Improve `verify_local_windows.ps1` with prerequisite checks:
    - Python version, venv activation check, pip/pytest presence,
    - Node/npm version checks,
    - actionable EPERM diagnosis + fallback flags (`-SkipFrontend` and split backend/frontend stages).
  - Update `README.md` with explicit recovery flow and expected outputs.
- Tests/validation:
  - Dry-run docs flow in a clean shell; ensure failures return clear next-step guidance.
- Done when:
  - Script fails fast with precise remediation, and docs match behavior.

**Chunk 5: Raise frontend workflow coverage + add one E2E journey (Medium)**
- Scope: move beyond route-redirect-only test in `frontend/src/App.test.tsx`.
- Changes:
  - Add integration tests around `frontend/src/pages/AppPortal.tsx` role rendering and key workflow interactions (notifications read, admin data-quality view switch).
  - Add dashboard-level tests for at least student + one operational role.
  - Add one E2E path (login ΓåÆ role dashboard ΓåÆ perform one core action) using Playwright (recommended) under `frontend/e2e`.
- Tests:
  - `npm run test` includes new integration suites.
  - E2E command added to `frontend/package.json` and documented in `README.md`.
- Done when:
  - Role-specific user journeys have regression protection beyond routing.

**Execution order**
1) Chunk 1 (quickest risk reduction)  
2) Chunk 2 (largest architecture delta)  
3) Chunk 3 (cross-cutting enforcement)  
4) Chunk 4 (verification reliability)  
5) Chunk 5 (frontend confidence)

- Goal: block non-student users from self-service registration writes to close the High-severity auth gap.

**Implementation Steps**

- Call `_require_student(user)` at the start of:

  - `waitlist(...)`

**Acceptance Criteria**
- Non-student roles always receive 403 on registration write endpoints.
- Student happy-path enrollment flow remains green.

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.security import hash_password
from app.models.access import ScopeGrant, ScopeType
from app.models.admin import Course, Organization, RegistrationRound, Section, Term
from app.models.registration import AddDropRequest, Enrollment, EnrollmentStatus
from app.models.user import User, UserRole


def _create_user(db: Session, username: str, role: UserRole, password: str, org_id: int | None = None) -> User:
    user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password(password), role=role, is_active=True, org_id=org_id)
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(user)
    return user

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.core.security import hash_password
from app.models.access import ScopeGrant, ScopeType
from app.models.admin import Course, Organization, RegistrationRound, Section, Term
from app.models.registration import AddDropRequest, Enrollment, EnrollmentStatus
from app.models.user import User, UserRole


def _create_user(db: Session, username: str, role: UserRole, password: str, org_id: int | None = None) -> User:
    user = User(username=username, password_hash=hash_password(password), role=role, is_active=True, org_id=org_id)
    db.add(user)
    db.commit()
    db.refresh(user)
    return user


def _login(client, username: str, password: str) -> dict[str, str]:
    response = client.post("/api/v1/auth/login", json={"username": username, "password": password})
    assert response.status_code == 200
    return {"Authorization": f"Bearer {response.json()['token']}"}


def _seed_catalog(db: Session, with_active_round: bool = True, org_code: str = "ORG1") -> tuple[int, int, int, int, int]:
    organization = Organization(name=f"Org {org_code}", code=org_code, is_active=True)
    db.add(organization)
    db.flush()
    term = Term(organization_id=organization.id, name="Fall 2026", starts_on="2026-09-01", ends_on="2026-12-20", is_active=True)
    db.add(term)
    db.flush()
    prereq_course = Course(organization_id=organization.id, code="MATH100", title="Math", credits=3, prerequisites=[])
    target_course = Course(organization_id=organization.id, code="CS200", title="Systems", credits=3, prerequisites=["MATH100"])
    db.add(prereq_course)
    db.add(target_course)
    db.flush()
    prereq_section = Section(course_id=prereq_course.id, term_id=term.id, code="P1", instructor_id=None, capacity=30)
    target_section = Section(course_id=target_course.id, term_id=term.id, code="S1", instructor_id=None, capacity=1)
    db.add(prereq_section)
    db.add(target_section)
    if with_active_round:
        db.add(
            RegistrationRound(
                term_id=term.id,
                name="Primary",
                starts_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=1),
                ends_at=datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(hours=3),
                is_active=True,
            )
        )
    db.commit()
    return organization.id, prereq_course.id, target_course.id, prereq_section.id, target_section.id


def _grant_org_scope(db: Session, user_id: int, org_id: int) -> None:
    db.add(ScopeGrant(user_id=user_id, scope_type=ScopeType.organization, scope_id=org_id))
    db.commit()


def test_course_discovery_and_eligibility(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, target_course_id, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student = _create_user(db_session, "stu1", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "stu1", "StudentPass123!")
    courses = client.get("/api/v1/courses", headers=headers)
    assert courses.status_code == 200
    assert len(courses.json()) >= 1

    detail = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}", headers=headers)
    assert detail.status_code == 200
    assert detail.json()["code"] == "CS200"

    eligibility = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}/sections/{target_section_id}/eligibility", headers=headers)
    assert eligibility.status_code == 200
    assert eligibility.json()["eligible"] is True


def test_enroll_idempotency_and_conflict(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, target_course_id, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student = _create_user(db_session, "stu2", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "stu2", "StudentPass123!")
    enroll_headers = {**headers, "Idempotency-Key": "enroll-key-1"}
    first = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200
    assert first.json()["status"] in {"enrolled", "already_enrolled"}

    replay = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert replay.status_code == 200
    assert replay.json() == first.json()

    bad_reuse = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id + 999}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert bad_reuse.status_code == 403

    another_student = _create_user(db_session, "stu3", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=another_student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()
    headers_2 = _login(client, "stu3", "StudentPass123!")
    full = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_2, "Idempotency-Key": "enroll-key-2"},
    )
    assert full.status_code == 409


def test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student_a = _create_user(db_session, "stu4", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    student_b = _create_user(db_session, "stu5", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_a.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_b.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers_a = _login(client, "stu4", "StudentPass123!")
    headers_b = _login(client, "stu5", "StudentPass123!")

    enroll_a = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-path-a"},
    )
    assert enroll_a.status_code == 200

    wait_b = client.post("/api/v1/registration/waitlist", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=headers_b)
    assert wait_b.status_code == 200
    assert wait_b.json()["status"] in {"waitlisted", "already_waitlisted"}

    db_session.commit()
    drop_a = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/drop",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-key-a"},
    )
    assert drop_a.status_code == 200
    db_session.expire_all()

    status_b = client.get("/api/v1/registration/status", headers=headers_b)
    assert status_b.status_code == 200
    assert any(item["section_id"] == target_section_id and item["status"] == "ENROLLED" for item in status_b.json())

    history_b = client.get("/api/v1/registration/history", headers=headers_b)
    assert history_b.status_code == 200
    assert len(history_b.json()) > 0


def test_idempotency_key_expires_after_24h(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student_a = _create_user(db_session, "stu_idm_a", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    student_b = _create_user(db_session, "stu_idm_b", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_a.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_b.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers_a = _login(client, "stu_idm_a", "StudentPass123!")
    headers_b = _login(client, "stu_idm_b", "StudentPass123!")

    first_enroll = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "seat-holder"},
    )
    assert first_enroll.status_code == 200

    key = "window-key"
    full_attempt = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_b, "Idempotency-Key": key},
    )
    assert full_attempt.status_code == 409

    stored = (
        db_session.query(AddDropRequest)
        .filter(
            AddDropRequest.actor_id == student_b.id,
            AddDropRequest.operation == "ENROLL",
            AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == key,
        )
        .first()
    )
    assert stored is not None
    stored.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=25)
    db_session.commit()

    drop_holder = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/drop",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-seat-holder"},
    )
    assert drop_holder.status_code == 200

    reused_after_window = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_b, "Idempotency-Key": key},
    )
    assert reused_after_window.status_code == 200
    assert reused_after_window.json()["status"] == "enrolled"


def test_eligibility_missing_prereq_and_missing_idempotency(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, target_course_id, _, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    _create_user(db_session, "stu6", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    headers = _login(client, "stu6", "StudentPass123!")

    eligibility = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}/sections/{target_section_id}/eligibility", headers=headers)
    assert eligibility.status_code == 200
    assert eligibility.json()["eligible"] is False

    enroll_missing_header = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=headers)
    assert enroll_missing_header.status_code == 400


def test_course_discovery_requires_auth(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    _, _, target_course_id, _, _ = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORGAUTH")
    courses = client.get("/api/v1/courses")
    assert courses.status_code == 401
    details = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}")
    assert details.status_code == 401


def test_registration_scope_denies_cross_org_enrollment(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org1_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORG-A")
    org2_id, _, _, _, _ = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORG-B")
    student = _create_user(db_session, "scope_stu", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org2_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "scope_stu", "StudentPass123!")
    denied = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers, "Idempotency-Key": "scope-denied"},
    )
    assert denied.status_code == 403

    finance_clerk = _create_user(db_session, "reg_clerk", UserRole.finance_clerk, "FinancePass1!", org_id=org2_id)
    _grant_org_scope(db_session, finance_clerk.id, org1_id)
    clerk_headers = _login(client, "reg_clerk", "FinancePass1!")
    allowed_courses = client.get("/api/v1/courses", headers=clerk_headers)
    assert allowed_courses.status_code == 200
    assert len(allowed_courses.json()) >= 1

def _grant_org_scope(db: Session, user_id: int, org_id: int) -> None:
    db.add(ScopeGrant(user_id=user_id, scope_type=ScopeType.organization, scope_id=org_id))
    db.commit()


def test_course_discovery_and_eligibility(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, target_course_id, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student = _create_user(db_session, "stu1", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "stu1", "StudentPass123!")
    courses = client.get("/api/v1/courses", headers=headers)
    assert courses.status_code == 200
    assert len(courses.json()) >= 1

    detail = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}", headers=headers)
    assert detail.status_code == 200
    assert detail.json()["code"] == "CS200"

    eligibility = client.get(f"/api/v1/courses/{target_course_id}/sections/{target_section_id}/eligibility", headers=headers)
    assert eligibility.status_code == 200
    assert eligibility.json()["eligible"] is True


def test_enroll_idempotency_and_conflict(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, target_course_id, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student = _create_user(db_session, "stu2", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "stu2", "StudentPass123!")
    enroll_headers = {**headers, "Idempotency-Key": "enroll-key-1"}
    first = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert first.status_code == 200
    assert first.json()["status"] in {"enrolled", "already_enrolled"}

    replay = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert replay.status_code == 200
    assert replay.json() == first.json()

    bad_reuse = client.post("/api/v1/registration/enroll", json={"section_id": target_section_id + 999}, headers=enroll_headers)
    assert bad_reuse.status_code == 403

    another_student = _create_user(db_session, "stu3", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=another_student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()
    headers_2 = _login(client, "stu3", "StudentPass123!")
    full = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_2, "Idempotency-Key": "enroll-key-2"},
    )
    assert full.status_code == 409


def test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student_a = _create_user(db_session, "stu4", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    student_b = _create_user(db_session, "stu5", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_a.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_b.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers_a = _login(client, "stu4", "StudentPass123!")
    headers_b = _login(client, "stu5", "StudentPass123!")

    enroll_a = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-path-a"},
    )
    assert enroll_a.status_code == 200

    wait_b = client.post("/api/v1/registration/waitlist", json={"section_id": target_section_id}, headers=headers_b)
    assert wait_b.status_code == 200
    assert wait_b.json()["status"] in {"waitlisted", "already_waitlisted"}

    db_session.commit()
    drop_a = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/drop",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-key-a"},
    )
    assert drop_a.status_code == 200
    db_session.expire_all()

    status_b = client.get("/api/v1/registration/status", headers=headers_b)
    assert status_b.status_code == 200
    assert any(item["section_id"] == target_section_id and item["status"] == "ENROLLED" for item in status_b.json())

    history_b = client.get("/api/v1/registration/history", headers=headers_b)
    assert history_b.status_code == 200
    assert len(history_b.json()) > 0


def test_idempotency_key_expires_after_24h(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session)
    student_a = _create_user(db_session, "stu_idm_a", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    student_b = _create_user(db_session, "stu_idm_b", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_a.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student_b.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers_a = _login(client, "stu_idm_a", "StudentPass123!")
    headers_b = _login(client, "stu_idm_b", "StudentPass123!")

    first_enroll = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "seat-holder"},
    )
    assert first_enroll.status_code == 200

    key = "window-key"
    full_attempt = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_b, "Idempotency-Key": key},
    )
    assert full_attempt.status_code == 409

    stored = (
        db_session.query(AddDropRequest)
        .filter(
            AddDropRequest.actor_id == student_b.id,
            AddDropRequest.operation == "ENROLL",
            AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == key,
        )
        .first()
    )
    assert stored is not None
    stored.created_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=25)
    db_session.commit()

    drop_holder = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/drop",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_a, "Idempotency-Key": "drop-seat-holder"},
    )
    assert drop_holder.status_code == 200

    reused_after_window = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers_b, "Idempotency-Key": key},
    )
    assert reused_after_window.status_code == 200
    assert reused_after_window.json()["status"] == "enrolled"


def test_eligibility_missing_prereq_and_missing_idempotency(client, db_session: Session) -> None:

def test_registration_writes_forbidden_for_non_student_roles(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org_id, _, _, _, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORGROLE")

    actor_specs = [
        ("reg_admin", UserRole.admin, "AdminPass123!"),
        ("reg_instructor", UserRole.instructor, "InstructorPass123!"),
        ("reg_finance", UserRole.finance_clerk, "FinancePass123!"),
    ]

    for username, role, password in actor_specs:
        _create_user(db_session, username, role, password, org_id=org_id)
        headers = _login(client, username, password)

        enroll_response = client.post(
            "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
            json={"section_id": target_section_id},
            headers={**headers, "Idempotency-Key": f"enroll-{role.value}"},
        )
        assert enroll_response.status_code == 403

        waitlist_response = client.post(
            "/api/v1/registration/waitlist",
            json={"section_id": target_section_id},
            headers=headers,
        )
        assert waitlist_response.status_code == 403

        drop_response = client.post(
            "/api/v1/registration/drop",
            json={"section_id": target_section_id},
            headers={**headers, "Idempotency-Key": f"drop-{role.value}"},
        )
        assert drop_response.status_code == 403


def test_registration_scope_denies_cross_org_enrollment(client, db_session: Session) -> None:
    org1_id, _, _, prereq_section_id, target_section_id = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORG-A")
    org2_id, _, _, _, _ = _seed_catalog(db_session, org_code="ORG-B")
    student = _create_user(db_session, "scope_stu", UserRole.student, "StudentPass123!", org_id=org2_id)
    db_session.add(Enrollment(student_id=student.id, section_id=prereq_section_id, status=EnrollmentStatus.completed))
    db_session.commit()

    headers = _login(client, "scope_stu", "StudentPass123!")
    denied = client.post(
        "/api/v1/registration/enroll",
        json={"section_id": target_section_id},
        headers={**headers, "Idempotency-Key": "scope-denied"},
    )
    assert denied.status_code == 403

    finance_clerk = _create_user(db_session, "reg_clerk", UserRole.finance_clerk, "FinancePass1!", org_id=org2_id)
    _grant_org_scope(db_session, finance_clerk.id, org1_id)

    allowed_courses = client.get("/api/v1/courses", headers=clerk_headers)
    assert allowed_courses.status_code == 200
    assert len(allowed_courses.json()) >= 1

 def _request_hash(payload: dict) -> str:

+def _to_utc(value: datetime) -> datetime:
+    if value.tzinfo is None:
+        return value.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
+    return value.astimezone(timezone.utc)
+
+
+def _purge_expired_idempotency_key(db: Session, actor_id: int, operation: str, idempotency_key: str) -> None:
+    cutoff = _utcnow() - timedelta(hours=IDEMPOTENCY_WINDOW_HOURS)
+    rows = (
+        db.query(AddDropRequest)
+        .filter(
+            AddDropRequest.actor_id == actor_id,
+            AddDropRequest.operation == operation,
+            AddDropRequest.idempotency_key == idempotency_key,
+        )
+        .all()
+    )
+    deleted_any = False
+    for row in rows:
+        if _to_utc(row.created_at) < cutoff:
+            db.delete(row)
+            deleted_any = True
+    if deleted_any:
+        db.flush()
+
