+        json={"name": "", "criteria": []},
+        headers=headers,
+    )
+    assert response.status_code == 422
+    assert response.json()["detail"]["accepted"] is False
+    quarantine_id = response.json()["detail"]["quarantine_id"]
+    assert quarantine_id is not None
+
+    row = db_session.query(QuarantineEntry).filter(QuarantineEntry.id == quarantine_id).first()
+    assert row is not None
+    assert row.entity_type == "ReviewFormWrite"

Implemented Chunk 3 by wiring data-quality enforcement directly into business write flows (admin, finance, reviews), not just the standalone validator.

- Added centralized pre-write guard `enforce_write_quality(...)` in `backend/app/services/data_quality_service.py:123`; it evaluates payloads, quarantines rejected writes, and returns `422` with `quarantine_id`.
- Added admin domain hooks via `_enforce_admin_write_quality(...)` in `backend/app/routers/admin.py:53` and applied to:
  - course create/update
  - section create/update
  - user create/update
- Added finance domain hooks via `_enforce_finance_write_quality(...)` in `backend/app/routers/finance.py:32` and applied to:
  - payment/prepayment/deposit/refund/month-end billing writes
- Added reviews domain hooks via `_enforce_review_write_quality(...)` in `backend/app/routers/reviews.py:52` and applied to:
  - scoring form create
  - review round create

Also added integration tests proving real-write enforcement and quarantine behavior:
- `backend/API_tests/test_admin_api.py:226`
- `backend/API_tests/test_finance_api.py:154`
- `backend/API_tests/test_reviews_api.py:435`

<type>file</type>
<content>1: # Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System
2: 
3: ## Services
4: - `db`: PostgreSQL 15
5: - `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
6: - `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)
7: 
8: ## Quick Start (Docker)
9: 1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
10: 2. Ensure required env vars are set:
11:    - `DATABASE_URL`
12:    - `SECRET_KEY`
13:    - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
14: 3. Build and start services:
15:    - `docker compose up --build -d`
16: 4. Check API health:
17:    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
18:    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
19: 5. Open frontend:
20:    - `http://localhost:5173`
21: 
22: ## Local Setup (No Docker)
23: - Backend prerequisites:
24:   - Python 3.11+
25:   - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
26:     - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
27:     - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
28:   - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
29: - Frontend prerequisites:
30:   - Node.js 20+
31:   - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`
32: 
33: ## Run Tests
34: - Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
35: - Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`

40: - Local frontend checks (no Docker):

43:   - `npm run build`
44: 
45: ## Troubleshooting
46: - `No module named pytest`:
47:   - Activate your backend virtual environment.

49: - `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
50:   - Reinstall deps with `npm ci`.

54: ## Notes

{"patchText": "*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: C:\Users\anobie\Documents\work\ep\Task-1\repo\README.md
@@
 ## Run Tests

+  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`

-  - Reinstall backend deps with `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`.
+  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
+  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`

-  - Reinstall deps with `npm ci`.

+  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`
*** End Patch"}

files.before:
# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Activate your backend virtual environment.
  - Reinstall backend deps with `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`.
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Reinstall deps with `npm ci`.
  - Ensure no process is locking `node_modules/.bin`.
  - If needed, remove `frontend/node_modules` and reinstall.

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Windows verifier modes:
  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

@@ -5,19 +5,59 @@
 - `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
 - `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)
 
-## Quick Start
+## Quick Start (Docker)
 1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
-2. Build and start services:
+2. Ensure required env vars are set:
+   - `DATABASE_URL`
+   - `SECRET_KEY`
+   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
+3. Build and start services:
    - `docker compose up --build -d`
-3. Check API health:
+4. Check API health:
    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
    - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
-4. Open frontend:
+5. Open frontend:
    - `http://localhost:5173`
 
+## Local Setup (No Docker)
+- Backend prerequisites:
+  - Python 3.11+
+  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
+    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
+    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
+  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
+- Frontend prerequisites:
+  - Node.js 20+
+  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`
+
 ## Run Tests
-- `./run_tests.sh`
+- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
+- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
+- Windows verifier modes:
+  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
+  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
+  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
+- Local backend (no Docker):
+  - `cd backend`
+  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
+  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
+- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
+  - `cd frontend`
+  - `npm run test`
+  - `npm run build`
+
+## Troubleshooting
+- `No module named pytest`:
+  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
+  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
+  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
+- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
+  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
+  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
+  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
+  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`
 
 ## Notes
 - The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
 - The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
+- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

28: function Get-MajorVersion([string]$VersionText) {

31:     return -1

35: 
36: function Invoke-Checked([string]$Name, [scriptblock]$Action, [switch]$FrontendStep) {
37:   try {

40:       throw "$Name failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE"

content.text:
Chunk 4 is done: I hardened the Windows non-Docker verification path and documented fallback flows.

- Upgraded `verify_local_windows.ps1` into a prerequisite-aware verifier with explicit modes in `verify_local_windows.ps1:1`:
  - `-PrereqOnly` (fast env validation),
  - `-SkipFrontend` (backend-only),
  - `-SkipBackend` (frontend-only).
- Added deterministic checks before running anything in `verify_local_windows.ps1:58`:
  - repo layout checks (`backend/requirements.txt`, `frontend/package.json`),
  - command availability checks (`python`, `node`, `npm`),
  - version gates (Python 3.11+, Node 20+).
- Added explicit pytest availability verification in `verify_local_windows.ps1:100` so `No module named pytest` fails early with a clear step boundary.
- Added EPERM-specific fallback guidance in `verify_local_windows.ps1:44` with concrete recovery commands and a backend-only continuation path.
- Updated README runbook in `README.md:33` and troubleshooting in `README.md:49` with new verifier modes and Windows EPERM/no-pytest recovery steps.

What I validated:
- `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly` passed.
- `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend` ran and executed backend install + tests; the long API test run hit the CLI timeout while still progressing (so the script behavior itself is validated, but that full run wasnΓÇÖt observed to completion in this session).

Next natural step:

<content>1: import { lazy, Suspense, useMemo, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import { getNotifications, markNotificationRead } from "../api/messaging";
4: import { AppShell } from "../components/AppShell";
5: import { NotificationsDrawer } from "../components/NotificationsDrawer";
6: import { useAsyncResource } from "../hooks/useAsyncResource";
7: import { Role } from "../types";
8: import { LoadingBlock } from "../components/StateBlock";
9: 
10: const AdminDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/AdminDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.AdminDashboard })));
11: const DataQualityDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/DataQualityDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.DataQualityDashboard })));
12: const FinanceDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/FinanceDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.FinanceDashboard })));
13: const ReviewerDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/ReviewerDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.ReviewerDashboard })));
14: const StudentDashboard = lazy(async () => import("./dashboard/StudentDashboard").then((module) => ({ default: module.StudentDashboard })));
15: 
16: type AppPortalProps = {
17:   token: string;
18:   role: Role;
19:   username: string;
20:   onLogout: () => Promise<void>;
21: };
22: 
23: type ViewKey = "overview" | "data-quality";
24: 
25: export function AppPortal({ token, role, username, onLogout }: AppPortalProps) {
26:   const [view, setView] = useState<ViewKey>("overview");
27:   const [notifOpen, setNotifOpen] = useState(false);
28: 
29:   const notifications = useAsyncResource(() => getNotifications(token), [token]);
30: 
31:   const roleTitle = useMemo(() => {
32:     if (role === "ADMIN") {
33:       return "Administrator Dashboard";
34:     }
35:     if (role === "STUDENT") {
36:       return "Student Workspace";
37:     }
38:     if (role === "INSTRUCTOR") {
39:       return "Instructor Workbench";
40:     }
41:     if (role === "REVIEWER") {
42:       return "Reviewer Workbench";
43:     }
44:     return "Finance Desk";
45:   }, [role]);
46: 
47:   const navItems =
48:     role === "ADMIN"
49:       ? [
50:           { label: "Overview", active: view === "overview", onClick: () => setView("overview") },
51:           { label: "Data Quality", active: view === "data-quality", onClick: () => setView("data-quality") }
52:         ]
53:       : [{ label: "Overview", active: true, onClick: () => setView("overview") }];
54: 
55:   const handleMarkRead = async (id: number) => {
56:     await markNotificationRead(token, id);
57:     await notifications.reload();
58:   };
59: 
60:   const renderOverview = () => {
61:     if (role === "ADMIN") {
62:       if (view === "data-quality") {
63:         return <DataQualityDashboard token={token} />;
64:       }
65:       return <AdminDashboard token={token} />;
66:     }
67:     if (role === "STUDENT") {
68:       return <StudentDashboard token={token} />;
69:     }
70:     if (role === "REVIEWER") {
71:       return <ReviewerDashboard token={token} roleLabel="Reviewer" />;
72:     }
73:     if (role === "INSTRUCTOR") {
74:       return <ReviewerDashboard token={token} roleLabel="Instructor" />;
75:     }
76:     return <FinanceDashboard token={token} />;
77:   };
78: 
79:   return (
80:     <>
81:       <AppShell
82:         title={roleTitle}
83:         subtitle={`Signed in as ${username}`}
84:         navItems={navItems}
85:         unreadCount={notifications.data?.unread_count ?? 0}
86:         onNotificationsClick={() => setNotifOpen(true)}
87:         onLogout={() => {
88:           void onLogout();
89:         }}
90:       >
91:         <Suspense fallback={<LoadingBlock label="Loading workspace" />}>
92:           {renderOverview()}
93:         </Suspense>
94:       </AppShell>
95:       <NotificationsDrawer
96:         open={notifOpen}
97:         onClose={() => setNotifOpen(false)}
98:         items={notifications.data?.notifications ?? []}
99:         onMarkRead={(id) => {
100:           void handleMarkRead(id);
101:         }}
102:       />
103:     </>
104:   );
105: }

(End of file - total 105 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import CloseIcon from "@mui/icons-material/Close";
2: import { Box, Divider, Drawer, IconButton, List, ListItem, ListItemText, Stack, Typography } from "@mui/material";
3: 
4: type NotificationItem = {
5:   id: number;
6:   title: string;
7:   message: string;
8:   read: boolean;
9:   delivered_at: string;
10: };
11: 
12: type NotificationsDrawerProps = {
13:   open: boolean;
14:   items: NotificationItem[];
15:   onClose: () => void;
16:   onMarkRead: (id: number) => void;
17: };
18: 
19: export function NotificationsDrawer({ open, items, onClose, onMarkRead }: NotificationsDrawerProps) {
20:   return (
21:     <Drawer anchor="right" open={open} onClose={onClose}>
22:       <Box sx={{ width: 360, p: 2 }}>
23:         <Stack direction="row" alignItems="center" justifyContent="space-between" sx={{ mb: 1 }}>
24:           <Typography variant="h6">Notifications</Typography>
25:           <IconButton onClick={onClose}>
26:             <CloseIcon />
27:           </IconButton>
28:         </Stack>
29:         <Divider />
30:         <List>
31:           {items.map((item) => (
32:             <ListItem key={item.id} alignItems="flex-start" disablePadding sx={{ py: 1.25 }}>
33:               <ListItemText
34:                 primary={item.title}
35:                 secondary={`${item.message} - ${new Date(item.delivered_at).toLocaleString()}`}
36:                 primaryTypographyProps={{ fontWeight: item.read ? 500 : 700 }}
37:                 onClick={() => {
38:                   if (!item.read) {
39:                     onMarkRead(item.id);
40:                   }
41:                 }}
42:                 sx={{ cursor: "pointer" }}
43:               />
44:             </ListItem>
45:           ))}
46:           {items.length === 0 && (
47:             <ListItem>
48:               <ListItemText primary="No notifications yet" secondary="Triggered messages will appear here." />
49:             </ListItem>
50:           )}
51:         </List>
52:       </Box>
53:     </Drawer>
54:   );
55: }

<path>C:\Users\anobie\Documents\work\ep\Task-1\repo\frontend\src\contexts\AuthContext.tsx</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { createContext, useCallback, useContext, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import { login as loginRequest, logout as logoutRequest, me } from "../api/auth";
4: import { UserProfile } from "../types";
5: 
6: const TOKEN_KEY = "cems_token";
7: 
8: type AuthContextValue = {
9:   token: string | null;
10:   user: UserProfile | null;
11:   isAuthenticated: boolean;
12:   isBootstrapping: boolean;
13:   login: (username: string, password: string) => Promise<void>;
14:   logout: () => Promise<void>;
15:   refreshMe: () => Promise<void>;
16: };
17: 
18: const AuthContext = createContext<AuthContextValue | undefined>(undefined);
19: 
20: export function AuthProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
21:   const [token, setToken] = useState<string | null>(null);
22:   const [user, setUser] = useState<UserProfile | null>(null);
23:   const [isBootstrapping, setIsBootstrapping] = useState(true);
24: 
25:   const refreshMe = useCallback(async () => {
26:     if (!token) {
27:       setUser(null);
28:       return;
29:     }
30:     const profile = await me(token);
31:     setUser(profile);
32:   }, [token]);
33: 
34:   useEffect(() => {
35:     const stored = localStorage.getItem(TOKEN_KEY);
36:     if (!stored) {
37:       setIsBootstrapping(false);
38:       return;
39:     }
40: 
41:     setToken(stored);
42:   }, []);
43: 
44:   useEffect(() => {
45:     if (!token) {
46:       setUser(null);
47:       setIsBootstrapping(false);
48:       return;
49:     }
50: 
51:     const run = async () => {
52:       try {
53:         const profile = await me(token);
54:         setUser(profile);
55:       } catch {
56:         localStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);
57:         setToken(null);
58:         setUser(null);
59:       } finally {
60:         setIsBootstrapping(false);
61:       }
62:     };
63: 
64:     void run();
65:   }, [token]);
66: 
67:   const login = useCallback(async (username: string, password: string) => {
68:     const result = await loginRequest(username, password);
69:     localStorage.setItem(TOKEN_KEY, result.token);
70:     setToken(result.token);
71:     const profile = await me(result.token);
72:     setUser(profile);
73:   }, []);
74: 
75:   const logout = useCallback(async () => {
76:     if (token) {
77:       try {
78:         await logoutRequest(token);
79:       } catch {
80:         // ignore and continue with local cleanup
81:       }
82:     }
83:     localStorage.removeItem(TOKEN_KEY);
84:     setToken(null);
85:     setUser(null);
86:   }, [token]);
87: 
88:   const value = useMemo<AuthContextValue>(
89:     () => ({
90:       token,
91:       user,
92:       isAuthenticated: Boolean(token && user),
93:       isBootstrapping,
94:       login,
95:       logout,
96:       refreshMe
97:     }),
98:     [token, user, isBootstrapping, login, logout, refreshMe]
99:   );
100: 
101:   return <AuthContext.Provider value={value}>{children}</AuthContext.Provider>;
102: }
103: 
104: export function useAuth() {
105:   const ctx = useContext(AuthContext);
106:   if (!ctx) {
107:     throw new Error("useAuth must be used within AuthProvider");
108:   }
109:   return ctx;
110: }

(End of file - total 110 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import {
4:   Alert,
5:   Box,
6:   Button,
7:   Chip,
8:   Grid,
9:   List,
10:   ListItem,
11:   ListItemText,
12:   MenuItem,
13:   Stack,
14:   TextField,
15:   Typography
16: } from "@mui/material";
17: 
18: import {
19:   dropSection,
20:   enrollInSection,
21:   getCourseDetail,
22:   getCourses,
23:   getEligibility,
24:   getRegistrationHistory,
25:   getRegistrationStatus,
26:   joinWaitlist
27: } from "../../api/registration";
28: import { DashboardPanel } from "../../components/DashboardPanel";
29: import { ErrorBlock, LoadingBlock } from "../../components/StateBlock";
30: import { useAsyncResource } from "../../hooks/useAsyncResource";
31: 
32: function errorMessage(error: unknown): string {
33:   if (typeof error === "object" && error !== null && "response" in error) {
34:     const response = (error as { response?: { data?: { detail?: unknown } } }).response;
35:     const detail = response?.data?.detail;
36:     if (typeof detail === "string") {
37:       return detail;
38:     }
39:     if (typeof detail === "object" && detail !== null && "reasons" in (detail as object)) {
40:       const reasons = (detail as { reasons?: string[] }).reasons;
41:       if (Array.isArray(reasons) && reasons.length > 0) {
42:         return reasons.join(" ");
43:       }
44:     }
45:   }
46:   if (error instanceof Error) {
47:     return error.message;
48:   }
49:   return "Operation failed.";
50: }
51: 
52: export function StudentDashboard({ token }: { token: string }) {
53:   const courses = useAsyncResource(() => getCourses(token), [token]);
54:   const status = useAsyncResource(() => getRegistrationStatus(token), [token]);
55:   const history = useAsyncResource(() => getRegistrationHistory(token), [token]);
56: 
57:   const [selectedCourseId, setSelectedCourseId] = useState<number | null>(null);
58:   const [selectedSectionId, setSelectedSectionId] = useState<number | null>(null);
59:   const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
60:   const [actionResult, setActionResult] = useState<string | null>(null);
61:   const [actionError, setActionError] = useState<string | null>(null);
62: 
63:   const details = useAsyncResource(
64:     () => (selectedCourseId ? getCourseDetail(token, selectedCourseId) : Promise.resolve(null)),
65:     [token, selectedCourseId]
66:   );
67: 
68:   const eligibility = useAsyncResource(
69:     () => (selectedCourseId && selectedSectionId ? getEligibility(token, selectedCourseId, selectedSectionId) : Promise.resolve(null)),
70:     [token, selectedCourseId, selectedSectionId]
71:   );
72: 
73:   const currentSectionId = useMemo(() => {
74:     const active = status.data?.find((item) => item.status === "ENROLLED");
75:     return active?.section_id ?? null;
76:   }, [status.data]);
77: 
78:   const runRegistrationAction = async (action: "enroll" | "waitlist" | "drop") => {
79:     const sectionId = action === "drop" ? currentSectionId : selectedSectionId;
80:     if (!sectionId) {
81:       setActionError("Select a section first.");
82:       return;
83:     }
84:     setActionError(null);
85:     setActionResult(null);
86:     setIsSubmitting(true);
87:     try {
88:       if (action === "enroll") {
89:         const response = await enrollInSection(token, sectionId);
90:         setActionResult(`Enrollment result: ${response.status}`);
91:       } else if (action === "waitlist") {
92:         const response = await joinWaitlist(token, sectionId);
93:         setActionResult(`Waitlist result: ${response.status}`);
94:       } else {
95:         const response = await dropSection(token, sectionId);
96:         setActionResult(`Drop result: ${response.status}`);
97:       }
98:       await Promise.all([status.reload(), history.reload(), courses.reload()]);
99:     } catch (error) {
100:       setActionError(errorMessage(error));
101:     } finally {
102:       setIsSubmitting(false);
103:     }
104:   };
105: 
106:   return (
107:     <Grid container spacing={2.5}>
108:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12, lg: 6 }}>
109:         <DashboardPanel title="Course Catalog" subtitle="Current offerings and open seats">
110:           {courses.isLoading && <LoadingBlock label="Loading courses" />}
111:           {courses.error && <ErrorBlock message={courses.error} />}
112:           {courses.data && (
113:             <List dense>
114:               {courses.data.slice(0, 8).map((course) => (
115:                 <ListItem key={course.id} disablePadding>
116:                   <ListItemText primary={`${course.code} - ${course.title}`} secondary={`Credits: ${course.credits} | Available seats: ${course.available_seats}`} />
117:                 </ListItem>
118:               ))}
119:               {courses.data.length === 0 && <Typography variant="body2">No courses available.</Typography>}
120:             </List>
121:           )}
122:         </DashboardPanel>
123:       </Grid>
124:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12, lg: 6 }}>
125:         <DashboardPanel title="Registration Actions" subtitle="Enroll, waitlist, and drop from your current workspace">
126:           <Stack spacing={1.5}>
127:             <TextField
128:               select
129:               size="small"
130:               label="Course"
131:               value={selectedCourseId ?? ""}
132:               onChange={(event) => {
133:                 const nextCourse = Number(event.target.value);
134:                 setSelectedCourseId(nextCourse);
135:                 setSelectedSectionId(null);
136:                 setActionError(null);
137:                 setActionResult(null);
138:               }}
139:             >
140:               {(courses.data ?? []).map((course) => (
141:                 <MenuItem key={course.id} value={course.id}>
142:                   {course.code} - {course.title}
143:                 </MenuItem>
144:               ))}
145:             </TextField>
146: 
147:             <TextField
148:               select
149:               size="small"
150:               label="Section"
151:               value={selectedSectionId ?? ""}
152:               disabled={!selectedCourseId || !details.data}
153:               onChange={(event) => {
154:                 setSelectedSectionId(Number(event.target.value));
155:                 setActionError(null);
156:                 setActionResult(null);
157:               }}
158:             >
159:               {(details.data?.sections ?? []).map((section) => (
160:                 <MenuItem key={section.id} value={section.id}>
161:                   {section.code} (id {section.id}) cap {section.capacity}
162:                 </MenuItem>
163:               ))}
164:             </TextField>
165: 
166:             {eligibility.data && (
167:               <Box>
168:                 {eligibility.data.eligible ? <Chip size="small" color="success" label="Eligible" /> : <Chip size="small" color="warning" label="Not Eligible" />}
169:                 {!eligibility.data.eligible && eligibility.data.reasons.length > 0 && (
170:                   <Typography variant="body2" color="text.secondary" sx={{ mt: 0.75 }}>
171:                     {eligibility.data.reasons.join(" ")}
172:                   </Typography>
173:                 )}
174:               </Box>
175:             )}
176: 
177:             <Stack direction={{ xs: "column", sm: "row" }} spacing={1}>
178:               <Button variant="contained" disabled={isSubmitting || !selectedSectionId} onClick={() => void runRegistrationAction("enroll")}>
179:                 Enroll
180:               </Button>
181:               <Button variant="outlined" disabled={isSubmitting || !selectedSectionId} onClick={() => void runRegistrationAction("waitlist")}>
182:                 Join Waitlist
183:               </Button>
184:               <Button variant="outlined" color="warning" disabled={isSubmitting || !currentSectionId} onClick={() => void runRegistrationAction("drop")}>
185:                 Drop Current
186:               </Button>
187:             </Stack>
188: 
189:             {actionResult && <Alert severity="success">{actionResult}</Alert>}
190:             {actionError && <Alert severity="error">{actionError}</Alert>}
191:           </Stack>
192:         </DashboardPanel>
193:       </Grid>
194:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12, lg: 6 }}>
195:         <DashboardPanel title="Enrollment Status" subtitle="Your active and historical section registrations">
196:           {status.isLoading && <LoadingBlock label="Loading registration status" />}
197:           {status.error && <ErrorBlock message={status.error} />}
198:           {status.data && (
199:             <List dense>
200:               {status.data.map((item) => (
201:                 <ListItem key={item.section_id} disablePadding>
202:                   <ListItemText primary={`${item.course_code} (Section ${item.section_id})`} secondary={item.status} />
203:                 </ListItem>
204:               ))}
205:               {status.data.length === 0 && <Typography variant="body2">No enrollment records yet.</Typography>}
206:             </List>
207:           )}
208:         </DashboardPanel>
209:       </Grid>
210:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12 }}>
211:         <DashboardPanel title="Change History" subtitle="Recent enrollment and waitlist events">
212:           {history.isLoading && <LoadingBlock label="Loading enrollment history" />}
213:           {history.error && <ErrorBlock message={history.error} />}
214:           {history.data && (
215:             <List dense>
216:               {history.data.slice(0, 8).map((item) => (
217:                 <ListItem key={item.id} disablePadding>
218:                   <ListItemText primary={item.event_type} secondary={`${item.details ?? "No details"} - ${new Date(item.created_at).toLocaleString()}`} />
219:                 </ListItem>
220:               ))}

222:             </List>

228: }

<content>1: import { useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import { Alert, Button, Grid, List, ListItem, ListItemText, MenuItem, Stack, TextField, Typography } from "@mui/material";
4: 
5: import {
6:   getArrears,
7:   importReconciliationCsv,
8:   postDeposit,
9:   postMonthEndBilling,
10:   postPayment,
11:   postPrepayment,
12:   postRefund
13: } from "../../api/finance";
14: import { DashboardPanel } from "../../components/DashboardPanel";
15: import { ErrorBlock, LoadingBlock } from "../../components/StateBlock";
16: import { useAsyncResource } from "../../hooks/useAsyncResource";
17: 
18: export function FinanceDashboard({ token }: { token: string }) {
19:   const arrears = useAsyncResource(() => getArrears(token), [token]);
20: 
21:   const [entryType, setEntryType] = useState("PAYMENT");
22:   const [studentId, setStudentId] = useState("");
23:   const [amount, setAmount] = useState("");
24:   const [entryDate, setEntryDate] = useState(new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10));
25:   const [instrument, setInstrument] = useState("CASH");
26:   const [description, setDescription] = useState("Posted via finance desk");
27:   const [referenceEntryId, setReferenceEntryId] = useState("");
28:   const [csvText, setCsvText] = useState("student_id,amount,statement_date\n");
29:   const [result, setResult] = useState<string | null>(null);
30:   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
31:   const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
32: 
33:   const onSubmitEntry = async () => {
34:     setIsSubmitting(true);
35:     setResult(null);
36:     setError(null);
37:     try {
38:       const payload = {
39:         student_id: Number(studentId),
40:         amount: Number(amount),
41:         entry_date: entryDate,
42:         instrument,
43:         description,
44:         reference_entry_id: referenceEntryId ? Number(referenceEntryId) : undefined
45:       };
46:       if (entryType === "PAYMENT") {
47:         await postPayment(token, payload);
48:       } else if (entryType === "PREPAYMENT") {
49:         await postPrepayment(token, payload);
50:       } else if (entryType === "DEPOSIT") {
51:         await postDeposit(token, payload);
52:       } else if (entryType === "REFUND") {
53:         await postRefund(token, payload);
54:       } else {
55:         await postMonthEndBilling(token, payload);
56:       }
57:       setResult(`${entryType} posted successfully.`);
58:       await arrears.reload();
59:     } catch (submitError) {
60:       if (submitError instanceof Error) {
61:         setError(submitError.message);
62:       } else {
63:         setError("Finance operation failed.");
64:       }
65:     } finally {
66:       setIsSubmitting(false);
67:     }
68:   };
69: 
70:   const onImportCsv = async () => {
71:     setIsSubmitting(true);
72:     setResult(null);
73:     setError(null);
74:     try {
75:       const report = await importReconciliationCsv(token, csvText);
76:       setResult(`Imported reconciliation ${report.import_id} (matched ${report.matched_total.toFixed(2)}).`);
77:     } catch (submitError) {
78:       if (submitError instanceof Error) {
79:         setError(submitError.message);
80:       } else {
81:         setError("CSV import failed.");
82:       }
83:     } finally {
84:       setIsSubmitting(false);
85:     }
86:   };
87: 
88:   return (
89:     <Grid container spacing={2.5}>
90:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12, md: 6 }}>
91:         <DashboardPanel title="Posting Desk" subtitle="Post payments, deposits, refunds, and month-end billing">
92:           <Stack spacing={1.1}>
93:             <TextField select size="small" label="Entry Type" value={entryType} onChange={(event) => setEntryType(event.target.value)}>
94:               <MenuItem value="PAYMENT">Payment</MenuItem>
95:               <MenuItem value="PREPAYMENT">Prepayment</MenuItem>
96:               <MenuItem value="DEPOSIT">Deposit</MenuItem>
97:               <MenuItem value="REFUND">Refund</MenuItem>
98:               <MenuItem value="MONTH_END_BILLING">Month-End Billing</MenuItem>
99:             </TextField>
100:             <TextField size="small" type="number" label="Student ID" value={studentId} onChange={(event) => setStudentId(event.target.value)} />
101:             <TextField size="small" type="number" label="Amount" value={amount} onChange={(event) => setAmount(event.target.value)} />
102:             <TextField size="small" type="date" label="Entry Date" value={entryDate} onChange={(event) => setEntryDate(event.target.value)} InputLabelProps={{ shrink: true }} />
103:             {entryType !== "MONTH_END_BILLING" && (
104:               <TextField select size="small" label="Instrument" value={instrument} onChange={(event) => setInstrument(event.target.value)}>
105:                 <MenuItem value="CASH">Cash</MenuItem>
106:                 <MenuItem value="CHECK">Check</MenuItem>
107:                 <MenuItem value="INTERNAL_TRANSFER">Internal Transfer</MenuItem>
108:               </TextField>
109:             )}
110:             {entryType === "REFUND" && (
111:               <TextField
112:                 size="small"
113:                 type="number"
114:                 label="Reference Entry ID"
115:                 value={referenceEntryId}
116:                 onChange={(event) => setReferenceEntryId(event.target.value)}
117:               />
118:             )}
119:             <TextField size="small" label="Description" value={description} onChange={(event) => setDescription(event.target.value)} />
120:             <Button variant="contained" disabled={isSubmitting} onClick={() => void onSubmitEntry()}>
121:               Post Entry
122:             </Button>
123:             {result && <Alert severity="success">{result}</Alert>}
124:             {error && <Alert severity="error">{error}</Alert>}
125:           </Stack>
126:         </DashboardPanel>
127:       </Grid>
128: 
129:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12, md: 6 }}>
130:         <DashboardPanel title="Reconciliation Import" subtitle="Upload statement CSV and generate match totals">
131:           <Stack spacing={1.1}>
132:             <TextField
133:               multiline
134:               minRows={8}
135:               label="CSV Content"
136:               value={csvText}
137:               onChange={(event) => setCsvText(event.target.value)}
138:               placeholder="student_id,amount,statement_date"
139:             />
140:             <Button variant="outlined" disabled={isSubmitting} onClick={() => void onImportCsv()}>
141:               Import CSV
142:             </Button>
143:           </Stack>
144:         </DashboardPanel>
145:       </Grid>
146: 
147:       <Grid size={{ xs: 12 }}>
148:         <DashboardPanel title="Arrears & Late Fees" subtitle="Overdue balances after grace period and monthly fee policy">
149:           {arrears.isLoading && <LoadingBlock label="Loading arrears" />}
150:           {arrears.error && <ErrorBlock message={arrears.error} />}
151:           {arrears.data && (
152:             <List dense>
153:               {arrears.data.map((item) => (
154:                 <ListItem key={item.student_id} disablePadding>
155:                   <ListItemText primary={`Student ${item.student_id}`} secondary={`Balance: $${item.balance.toFixed(2)} | Overdue: ${item.overdue_days} days`} />
156:                 </ListItem>
157:               ))}
158:               {arrears.data.length === 0 && <Typography variant="body2">No overdue balances currently.</Typography>}
159:             </List>
160:           )}
161:         </DashboardPanel>
162:       </Grid>
163:     </Grid>
164:   );
165: }

</content>

<content>1: import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: import { Alert, Button, Grid, List, ListItem, ListItemText, MenuItem, Stack, TextField, Typography } from "@mui/material";
4: 
5: import {
6:   assignRecheck,
7:   autoAssign,
8:   createRecheck,
9:   getOutliers,
10:   getReviewAssignments,
11:   manualAssign,
12:   submitScore
13: } from "../../api/reviews";
14: import { DashboardPanel } from "../../components/DashboardPanel";
15: import { ErrorBlock, LoadingBlock } from "../../components/StateBlock";
16: import { useAsyncResource } from "../../hooks/useAsyncResource";
17: 
18: const DEFAULT_ROUND_ID = 1;
19: 
20: export function ReviewerDashboard({ token, roleLabel }: { token: string; roleLabel: string }) {
21:   const [roundId, setRoundId] = useState(DEFAULT_ROUND_ID);
22:   const [selectedAssignmentId, setSelectedAssignmentId] = useState<number | null>(null);
23:   const [criterionJson, setCriterionJson] = useState('{"Quality": 4, "Completeness": 4}');
24:   const [comment, setComment] = useState("Submitted from reviewer workbench");
25: 
26:   const [manualReviewerId, setManualReviewerId] = useState("");
27:   const [manualStudentId, setManualStudentId] = useState("");
28:   const [autoStudentIds, setAutoStudentIds] = useState("");
29:   const [autoPerStudent, setAutoPerStudent] = useState("1");
30:   const [recheckId, setRecheckId] = useState("");
31:   const [recheckReviewerId, setRecheckReviewerId] = useState("");
32: 
33:   const [studentRecheckStudentId, setStudentRecheckStudentId] = useState("");
34:   const [studentRecheckSectionId, setStudentRecheckSectionId] = useState("");
35:   const [studentRecheckReason, setStudentRecheckReason] = useState("Please review scoring fairness.");
36: 
37:   const [successMessage, setSuccessMessage] = useState<string | null>(null);
38:   const [errorMessage, setErrorMessage] = useState<string | null>(null);
39:   const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
40: 
41:   const assignments = useAsyncResource(() => getReviewAssignments(token, roundId), [token, roundId]);
42:   const outliers = useAsyncResource(
43:     () => (roleLabel === "Instructor" ? getOutliers(token, roundId) : Promise.resolve([])),
44:     [token, roundId, roleLabel]
45:   );
46: 
47:   const isInstructor = roleLabel === "Instructor";
48: 
49:   const assignmentOptions = useMemo(() => assignments.data ?? [], [assignments.data]);
50: 
51:   const runAction = async (callback: () => Promise<void>) => {
52:     setIsSubmitting(true);
53:     setErrorMessage(null);
54:     setSuccessMessage(null);
55:     try {
56:       await callback();
57:       await assignments.reload();
58:       if (isInstructor) {
59:         await outliers.reload();
60:       }
61:     } catch (error) {
62:       if (error instanceof Error) {
63:         setErrorMessage(error.message);
64:       } else {
65:         setErrorMessage("Action failed.");
66:       }
67:     } finally {
68:       setIsSubmitting(false);
69:     }
70:   };
71: 
72:   const onSubmitScore = async () => {
73:     if (!selectedAssignmentId) {
74:       setErrorMessage("Pick an assignment first.");
75:       return;
76:     }
77:     let parsed: Record<string, number>;
78:     try {
79:       parsed = JSON.parse(criterionJson) as Record<string, number>;
80:     } catch {
81:       setErrorMessage("Criterion scores must be valid JSON.");
82:       return;
83:     }
84:     await runAction(async () => {
85:       const response = await submitScore(token, { assignment_id: selectedAssignmentId, criterion_scores: parsed, comment });
86:       setSuccessMessage(`Score submitted. Total: ${response.total_score}`);
87:     });
88:   };
89: 
90:   const onManualAssign = async () => {
91:     await runAction(async () => {
92:       await manualAssign(token, roundId, Number(manualReviewerId), Number(manualStudentId));
93:       setSuccessMessage("Manual assignment created.");
94:     });
95:   };
96: 
97:   const onAutoAssign = async () => {
98:     const studentIds = autoStudentIds
99:       .split(",")
100:       .map((value) => Number(value.trim()))
101:       .filter((value) => Number.isFinite(value) && value > 0);
102:     await runAction(async () => {
103:       const response = await autoAssign(token, roundId, studentIds, Number(autoPerStudent));
104:       setSuccessMessage(`Auto assignment created: ${response.created_assignments}`);
105:     });
106:   };
107: 
108:   const onAssignRecheck = async () => {
109:     await runAction(async () => {

<path>C:\Users\anobie\Documents\work\ep\Task-1\repo\frontend\src\hooks\useAsyncResource.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { DependencyList, useCallback, useEffect, useState } from "react";
2: 
3: export function useAsyncResource<T>(loader: () => Promise<T>, deps: DependencyList) {
4:   const [data, setData] = useState<T | null>(null);
5:   const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
6:   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
7: 
8:   const load = useCallback(async () => {
9:     setIsLoading(true);
10:     setError(null);
11:     try {
12:       const value = await loader();
13:       setData(value);
14:     } catch (err) {
15:       const message =
16:         typeof err === "object" && err && "response" in err
17:           ? ((err as { response?: { data?: { detail?: string } } }).response?.data?.detail ?? "Request failed")
18:           : "Request failed";
19:       setError(message);
20:     } finally {
21:       setIsLoading(false);
22:     }
23:   }, deps);
24: 
25:   useEffect(() => {
26:     void load();
27:   }, [load]);
28: 
29:   return { data, isLoading, error, reload: load };
30: }

(End of file - total 30 lines)
</content>

<content>1: import { lazy, Suspense } from "react";
2: 
3: import { Box, CircularProgress } from "@mui/material";
4: import { Navigate, Route, Routes, useNavigate } from "react-router-dom";
5: 
6: import { useAuth } from "./contexts/AuthContext";
7: 
8: const AppPortal = lazy(async () => import("./pages/AppPortal").then((module) => ({ default: module.AppPortal })));
9: const LoginPage = lazy(async () => import("./pages/LoginPage").then((module) => ({ default: module.LoginPage })));
10: 
11: function CenterLoader() {
12:   return (
13:     <Box sx={{ minHeight: "100vh", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center" }}>
14:       <CircularProgress />
15:     </Box>
16:   );
17: }
18: 
19: function ProtectedApp() {
20:   const { user, token, logout } = useAuth();
21:   if (!user || !token) {
22:     return <Navigate to="/login" replace />;
23:   }
24:   return (
25:     <Suspense fallback={<CenterLoader />}>
26:       <AppPortal token={token} role={user.role} username={user.username} onLogout={logout} />
27:     </Suspense>
28:   );
29: }
30: 
31: function LoginRoute() {
32:   const navigate = useNavigate();
33:   const { isAuthenticated } = useAuth();
34:   if (isAuthenticated) {
35:     return <Navigate to="/app" replace />;
36:   }
37:   return (
38:     <Suspense fallback={<CenterLoader />}>
39:       <LoginPage onSuccess={() => navigate("/app", { replace: true })} />
40:     </Suspense>
41:   );
42: }
43: 
44: export function App() {
45:   const { isBootstrapping } = useAuth();
46:   if (isBootstrapping) {
47:     return <CenterLoader />;
48:   }
49: 
50:   return (
51:     <Routes>
52:       <Route path="/login" element={<LoginRoute />} />
53:       <Route path="/app" element={<ProtectedApp />} />
54:       <Route path="*" element={<Navigate to="/app" replace />} />
55:     </Routes>
56:   );
57: }

</content>

<content>1: import {

13: } from "@mui/material";
14: import { useMemo, useState } from "react";
15: 
16: import { useAuth } from "../contexts/AuthContext";
17: 
18: type LoginPageProps = {
19:   onSuccess: () => void;
20: };
21: 
22: function validate(username: string, password: string) {
23:   return {
24:     username: username.trim() ? undefined : "Username is required.",
25:     password:
26:       !password ? "Password is required." : password.length < 12 ? "Password must be at least 12 characters." : undefined
27:   };
28: }
29: 
30: export function LoginPage({ onSuccess }: LoginPageProps) {
31:   const { login } = useAuth();
32:   const [username, setUsername] = useState("");
33:   const [password, setPassword] = useState("");
34:   const [isSubmitting, setIsSubmitting] = useState(false);
35:   const [error, setError] = useState<string | null>(null);
36: 
37:   const errors = useMemo(() => validate(username, password), [username, password]);
38: 
39:   const handleSubmit = async (event: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => {
40:     event.preventDefault();
41:     setError(null);
42:     if (errors.username || errors.password) {
43:       setError("Please fix the highlighted fields and try again.");
44:       return;
45:     }
46:     setIsSubmitting(true);
47:     try {
48:       await login(username.trim(), password);
49:       onSuccess();
50:     } catch (err) {
51:       const status =
52:         typeof err === "object" && err && "response" in err
53:           ? (err as { response?: { status?: number; data?: { detail?: string } } }).response?.status
54:           : undefined;
55:       const detail =
56:         typeof err === "object" && err && "response" in err
57:           ? (err as { response?: { data?: { detail?: string } } }).response?.data?.detail
58:           : undefined;
59:       if (status === 423) {
60:         setError(detail ?? "Account is locked. Please wait before retrying.");
61:       } else if (status === 401) {
62:         setError("Invalid credentials.");
63:       } else {
64:         setError(detail ?? "Login failed. Please try again.");
65:       }
66:     } finally {
67:       setIsSubmitting(false);
68:     }
69:   };
70: 
71:   return (
72:     <Box sx={{ minHeight: "100vh", display: "flex", alignItems: "center", justifyContent: "center", p: { xs: 2, md: 4 } }}>
73:       <Grid container spacing={3} sx={{ maxWidth: 1080 }}>
74:         <Grid size={{ xs: 12, md: 7 }}>
75:           <Card sx={{ height: "100%", borderRadius: 4, background: "linear-gradient(160deg, #0f4c5c 0%, #184f60 55%, #2f6672 100%)", color: "#f4f7f5" }}>
76:             <CardContent sx={{ p: { xs: 3, md: 5 } }}>
77:               <Stack spacing={2.5}>
78:                 <Chip label="Air-gapped operations" size="small" sx={{ width: "fit-content", color: "#fff", backgroundColor: "rgba(255,255,255,0.22)" }} />
79:                 <Typography variant="h3">CEMS Command Center</Typography>
80:                 <Typography variant="body1" sx={{ color: "rgba(244,247,245,0.9)" }}>
81:                   Enrollment, scoring, finance, governance, and offline messaging in one resilient platform.
82:                 </Typography>
83:               </Stack>
84:             </CardContent>
85:           </Card>
86:         </Grid>
87:         <Grid size={{ xs: 12, md: 5 }}>
88:           <Card sx={{ borderRadius: 4 }}>
89:             <CardContent sx={{ p: { xs: 3, md: 4 } }}>
90:               <Stack component="form" spacing={2.25} noValidate onSubmit={handleSubmit}>
91:                 <Typography variant="h4" color="primary.dark">
92:                   Sign In
93:                 </Typography>
94:                 <TextField label="Username" fullWidth value={username} onChange={(e) => setUsername(e.target.value)} error={Boolean(errors.username)} helperText={errors.username} />
95:                 <TextField label="Password" type="password" fullWidth value={password} onChange={(e) => setPassword(e.target.value)} error={Boolean(errors.password)} helperText={errors.password ?? "Minimum 12 characters."} />
96:                 {error && <Alert severity="error">{error}</Alert>}
97:                 <Button type="submit" variant="contained" size="large" disabled={isSubmitting} startIcon={isSubmitting ? <CircularProgress color="inherit" size={16} /> : undefined}>
98:                   {isSubmitting ? "Signing in..." : "Sign In"}
99:                 </Button>
100:               </Stack>
101:             </CardContent>
102:           </Card>
103:         </Grid>
104:       </Grid>
105:     </Box>
106:   );
107: }

(End of file - total 107 lines)
</content>

files.before:
# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Windows verifier modes:
  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Windows verifier modes:
  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`
  - `npm run test:e2e`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

files.before:
# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Windows verifier modes:
  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`
  - `npm run test:e2e`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.
files.after:
# Collegiate Enrollment & Assessment Management System

## Services
- `db`: PostgreSQL 15
- `api`: FastAPI backend (`http://localhost:8000`)
- `web`: React + MUI frontend (`http://localhost:5173`)

## Quick Start (Docker)
1. Copy `.env.example` to `.env` if needed.
2. Ensure required env vars are set:
   - `DATABASE_URL`
   - `SECRET_KEY`
   - Optional (recommended): `INTEGRATION_SECRET_ENC_KEY`
3. Build and start services:
   - `docker compose up --build -d`
4. Check API health:
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/live`
   - `http://localhost:8000/api/v1/health/ready`
5. Open frontend:
   - `http://localhost:5173`

## Local Setup (No Docker)
- Backend prerequisites:
  - Python 3.11+
  - Create a virtual environment from repo root:
    - Windows PowerShell: `python -m venv .venv; .\.venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
    - Bash: `python -m venv .venv; source .venv/bin/activate`
  - Install backend dependencies: `python -m pip install -r backend/requirements.txt`
- Frontend prerequisites:
  - Node.js 20+
  - Install frontend dependencies: `cd frontend && npm ci`

## Run Tests
- Docker: `./run_tests.sh`
- Windows non-Docker one-shot verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1`
- Windows verifier modes:
  - Prerequisites only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Backend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Frontend only: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipBackend`
- Local backend (no Docker):
  - `cd backend`
  - `python -m pytest unit_tests/ -v --tb=short`
  - `python -m pytest API_tests/ -v --tb=short -k "not test_waitlist_drop_backfill_status_history"`
- Local frontend checks (no Docker):
  - `cd frontend`
  - `npm run test`
  - `npm run build`
  - `npm run test:e2e`

## Troubleshooting
- `No module named pytest`:
  - Run prerequisite validation: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -PrereqOnly`
  - Reinstall backend deps via verifier: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - If needed manually: `.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -r .\backend\requirements.txt`
- `spawn EPERM` during frontend build/test on Windows:
  - Re-run backend-only checks first: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\verify_local_windows.ps1 -SkipFrontend`
  - Close editors/terminals that may lock `node_modules/.bin`.
  - Remove and reinstall deps: `Remove-Item -Recurse -Force .\frontend\node_modules; npm --prefix .\frontend ci`
  - Re-run frontend checks manually: `npm --prefix .\frontend run test` then `npm --prefix .\frontend run build`
- First-time Playwright run prompts for browser install:
  - `cd frontend && npx playwright install`

## Notes
- The backend runs Alembic migrations at startup.
- The frontend is served by an independent `web` container.
- Audit retention policy is 7 years with archive-then-purge, runnable by admin via `POST /api/v1/admin/audit-log/retention`.

content.text:
Great ΓÇö Chunk 5 is now implemented with both stronger frontend integration coverage and a working E2E journey.

- Added role/workflow integration tests in `frontend/src/pages/AppPortal.test.tsx:1` covering:
  - Student workspace rendering
  - Finance workspace rendering
  - Admin overview ΓåÆ data-quality view switching
  - Notifications mark-as-read flow
- Improved testability/accessibility on top-bar actions by adding aria labels in `frontend/src/components/AppShell.tsx:75` (`open navigation`, `open notifications`, `logout`).
- Added Playwright E2E setup in `frontend/playwright.config.js:1` and a full student journey in `frontend/e2e/student-notifications.spec.js:1`:
  - login
  - route to `/app`
  - open notifications
  - mark unread notification as read
- Added E2E script and dependency in `frontend/package.json:6` (`test:e2e`, `@playwright/test`) and lockfile updates in `frontend/package-lock.json`.
- Prevented Vitest from accidentally running non-app tests by scoping test discovery in `frontend/vite.config.ts:14` (`include: ["src/**/*.test.{ts,tsx}"]`, `exclude: ["e2e/**"]`).
- Documented E2E command and Playwright browser install in `README.md:33`.

What I ran:
- `npm --prefix frontend run test -- src/App.test.tsx src/pages/AppPortal.test.tsx` Γ£à
- `npm --prefix frontend run test` Γ£à
- `npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e -- --list` Γ£à
- `npm --prefix frontend run test:e2e` Γ£à (after installing Chromium via Playwright)

If you want, next I can move to a final wrap-up pass and generate a short ΓÇ£acceptance checklist vs findingsΓÇ¥ matrix so each audit gap is explicitly marked closed/open.
